AlHamdulillah we completed the sessions on the miracles of Quan - and the purpose of all those sessions was to convince ourselves without the shadow of a doubt that the Quran could not possible be the word of a human being. We analyzed and judged the Quran from various different dimensions, and in each and everyone we found that it is impeccable. It is too perfect to be the word of a human being, because we cannot speak like that. We understand and recognize our limitations.

So now being convinced about the divinity of the Quran, the next step is to understand the purpose and the wisdoms of this message. What is our creator like? What does he want from us, and why? How does the Quran benefit us? What happens if we follow its teachings? What happens if we don't? How does Quran fit in into the design of creation? These are some of the many questions we will begin to answer in these sessions InshaAllah.

Now the key to understanding the Quran is the Arabic language itself! Not the present, distorted forms of "Spoken Arabic" Not even the grammatically accurate, "Proper Arabic". To truly understand the Quran we need to understand it in Ancient Arabic. Because ancient Arabic is not just "another" language out of the 6000 languages that exist today. The Arabic language is part of the revelation itself.
وَإِنَّهُۥ لَتَنزِيلُ رَبِّ ٱلْعَلَمِينَ
نَزَلَ بِهِ ٱلرُّوحُ ٱلْأَمِينُ
عَلَىٰ قَلْبِكَ لِتَكُونَ مِنَ ٱلْمُنذِرِينَ
Indeed this is a revelation from the Lord of the world; Sent down through the trustworthy Spirit; Upon your heart in order to warn people
بِلِسَانٍ عَرَبِىٍّۢ مُّبِينٍۢ
In the clear Arabic language.

If you remember, عجمۃ means to bite; but عربَۃ means to cut. And cutting is extremely precise in comparision to biting. So the very essence of the Arabic language is formed on precision. This is a language that was systematically perfected in order to hold the truths of revelation. The Quran was not revealed in one of the best languages that existed at the time. It's not like there were 50 languages out there, and then from those Allah some how picked Arabic because it was the best of what was available. Absolutely not! This language was specifically designed for the Quran. It was put in place and perfected before the time of the final messenger, with the ultimate purpose of capturing the perfection of the final revelaion.

I'll give you an example of some of the incredible nuances and subtlties in the Arabic language, so that you can really appreciate its depth; and get a good idea of how involved, and sophisticated this language is. Those of you who have studied the science of TAJWEED and MAKHARIJ from a proper MADRASA or a Maulvi Sahab. Those of you who know the proper pronunciation of Arabic letters, will truly appreciate this.

One of the most fascinating things about the Quran, is that its meanings go down to the letter itself. There is no other language that can do that. In other words, you can look up a word in the Arabic language, and understand some of its meaning, simply by looking at how its letters are pronounced.


So you take a word, for instance like نَفَسَ , NAFASAA is a word that is based on a tri-literal root, which is NAA and FAA and SA. But what's interesting is that as the last letter of the root changes, the meaning of the word changes, based on the attributes of the letter itself. So you have نَفَسَ which means to "blow or to breathe". And then you have نفث , "which is to blow very gently". Now if you observe the phenotic difference between how the letters س  and ث are pronounced; س as pronounced in a very normal tone; just like breathing! On the other hand, the letter ث is pronounced very softly. And so when the letter ث is added to the same root of Na and Faa. The meaning changes, "to blow softly". Simply because of the way the letter ث is pronounced. It's very soft.
And then you have نفح, which means a breeze; Breeze of the winds. So you get the ح sound . Which is in ریح, meaning winds. The sound that the winds make, is in the pronunciation of the letter ح itself. ریح. You can hear the wind in the letter ح. And then you have the word نفخ which is استعلاء. It is a very strong letter because of how the letter خ is pronounced. And because of the force in the letter KHA,  نفخ means to blow with force. This is incredible stuff. This is not a language that came out of ding-dong theory. There are all these theories based on which they believe languages were formed. There's the ding-dong theory, the Pooh-Pooh theory and the Bow-Wow theory. All other language evolved based on these theories - not the Arabic language. This is truly a divine language. It's amazing!

Now if you observe the sound that the letter خ makes, it is explosive. Like a blast. خ . And the sound contains friction in it. It has a bang to it. And that's why in the Quran you have the word, خلق, which also begins with the letter خ and it means to create. خلق means to create. As Allah said.

ٱلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَ ٱلسَّمَوَتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ
Allah is the one who created the heavens and the earth! What the word خلق? Because creation took place with a Big Bang. Right? Singularity exploded into this universe with the Big Bang. That is the خ in the word خلق.

So you have four words. نَفَسَ , nafaSA, nafaHA, and nafaKHA, all taking meaning from the sound of the last letter. And you will find this throughout the Arabic language. It is very sophisticated in the way that Allah has designed it. I mean you can't make this stuff up. It's just too incredible!! And these are signs of Allah. These are signs of Allah, placed into the very fabric of the one thing that was given to human beings, that was not give to any other creation. Language! Right?
سَنُرِيهِمْ آيَتِنَا فِى ٱلأٓفَاقِ وَفِىٓ أَنفُسِهِمْ حَتَّىٰ يَتَبَيَّنَ لَهُمْ أَنَّهُ ٱلْحَقُّ
We will show them, and keep showing them our signs, in the horizons and in their souls, until it becomes absolutely clear to them, that this message is the truth. These linguistic dimensions of the Quran are signs to prove that this is in fact a divine message.

So understanding Arabic, and specifically ancient Arabic is incredibly essential to understanding the Quran. And that's why we will study a lot of the terminology of the Quran linguistically. Because when the anceint Arab spoke, they painted pictures and sceneries with their words. It was like watching a movie. And that is my goal in these sessions, to help you see the movie of Quran.  InshaAllah by the end of these sessions, when you recite the Fateha, you will not just understand what it means, you'll actually be able to see a movie in your minds.

Adab
It is customary to always being the journey of knowledge with the topic of ADAB. Those of you who've read CHAHAL SABAQ, what's the first thing mentioned in it?
سب سے اچھی چیز کیا ہے؟ ادب
So let's understand the importance of ADAB before we begin our journey



The word for "beginning" in Arabic is called بَدَأَ; From the same root you get the word ابتدا, which means the 'start' or the 'beginning'. And we'll soon see in one of the future sessions, that one of the several meanings for the word "heart" (QALB) is "to reverse something". For example you have the word INQILAAB that comes from the same root QLB. What does INQILAAB mean? It means revolution. And what does revolution do? It reverses the order of things. So of the meanings of the word "heart" or "qlb" is to reverse something.
Now if you reverse the letters in the word بَدَأَ (which are BA DAL and ALIF), what do you get? You get ADAB! And so ADAB is the heart of knowledge. ADAB is a portal to the purification of the heart. It is the portal through which every spiritual journey begins. And for this reason, our Islamic tradition is so firmly rooted in this idea of ADAB.

ADAB is a difficult word to translate in English. It is generally translated as étiquettes or courtesy. The word ADEEB (which is a derivative of ADAB), means a "erudite person; someone who is learned, and has the best manners; someone who is very courteous". But in my opinion, a really good translation for the word ADAB is "comportment". 

I did not even know what that word meant when I first read it.  I thought it was that thing in a car in which you put your stuff (the glove comportment).

Comportment comes from two Latin words which means "how you carry yourself". The word "porter" is derived from it, because a porter is someone who carries your luggage. So comportment is "the way in which you carry yourself". And that's pretty much what ADAB is.

Now interestingly, ADAB is not something that is dictated by the SHAREEAH. The idea behind ADAB is that it is the most natural thing for a person. Because most of ADAB is simply common sense. 
Right after the battle of KHAYBER, which was faught against the Jews; a companion was asked to bring a notable lady from the Jewish community (صفية بنت حيي) to Rasul Allah (Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam). As this man brought her to the prophet's tent, he passed through the battle-ground of KHYBER, where she saw the dead body of her husband lying there. So she gets so distressed by seeing her husband in that state, she begins wailing. The prophet hears her cries, and he came out of his tent to see what happened, and he asks "What are you doing?".  The man says, I brought Sophia to you like you asked, and on the way she saw her dead husband and started crying. Rasul Allah said, "Couldn't you find another route to bring her to me?". And he said, "I did not think that you would mind". 
In other words, "what's the big deal?". Isn't that response interesting? Because this is our response to pretty much everything in life these days, right? What's the big deal. So what if I don't conver my head when I sit for dinner? What's the big deal if I don't say BISMILLAH.....

So the prophet looked at him and said, این رَحمَتُکَ؟ Where is your mercy? In other words, you don't need SHAREEAH to know this. You don't need revelation, to have basic common decency! And so a lot of ADAB is just that. It is a basic common decency. One of the hallmarks of Islamic learning is the relationship between students and teachers; or students and knowledge. It has always been a relationship of respect and compassion. The prophet said
لیس مِنَّا مَن لا یَرحَم صَغِیرَنَا.....  ولا یُوَقِّر کَبِیرَنا
He or she is not from among us, who does not have compassion for the young; and who does not respect the elderly. And KABEER does not necessarily mean age. It can means anything and everything that puts that thing in a high rank. Anything valuable or treasured is also called KABEER in Arabic. So knowledge (and especially divine knowledge) certainly falls in that category of KABEER. 

This is the reason why ADAB is so important in our times; because in this day and age, ADAB has become such an endangered specy. It is becoming completely extinct in our times. Children don't have ADAB for parents; students don't have ADAB for teachers, or knowledge. We barely follow the ADAAB of talking, eating, sleeping. And this is why you will find a lot of people in our DEEN who acquire this amazing knowledge, but you still find their hearts to be wretched. And if you trace the reason for that disconnect, you can almost inevitably trace it back to a lack of ADAB in acquiring this knowledge. Because this is not material knowledge! This is divine knowledge; this is celestial knowledge that comes from Allah(Subhanhu Wa Ta'ala). He bestows this gift upon the selected few he chooses. And the only reason he does that, is so that the receiver can take this knowledge; put it into practice; and through the combination of the two, purify their hearts with it. Rasul Allah (Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam) said…..
من عَمِلَ بِمَا عَلِمَ ..... وَرَّثَهُ اللهُ ما لَم يَعلَم
Whoever puts into practice what they learn, Allah will give them as an inheritance, knowledge of that which they did not know. And so people who fit this HADEES, they always put into practice what they learn. And as their knowledge increases their practice changes along with it. And what's so beautiful about this formula, is that we can always know what this person is learning, by witnessing what they are doing. So according to this hadees, when someone learns something and then applies it, their knowledge increases. But if one does not apply the proper ADAB while receiving this gift, this knowledge gets reduced to nothing more than academic information. It becomes something that is "good to know" (what we categorize as COOL). The knowledge never actually makes it to its destination, which is the heart. And there are many people around us that are living proofs of this fact.

And so we all must have courtesy with regard to Allah(Subhanhu Wa Ta'ala). We all need to behave properly with respect to His divine presence, if we wish to accomplish the goals of this knowledge. And the goal of this knowledge is the purification of our hearts. And make no mistake, there is a divine presence in these sessions. Because when Allah is called upon, he always answers!
وَإِذَا سَأَلَكَ عِبَادِى عَنِّى فَإِنِّى قَرِيبٌ
And when My slaves ask you, [O Muhammad], about Me - indeed I am near.
أُجِيبُ دَعْوَةَ ٱلدَّاعِ إِذَا دَعَانِ
I respond to the call of the supplicant when he calls upon Me.

And whats so incredible about this verse is that Allah did not say,
وَإِذَا سَأَلَكَ عِبَادِى عَنِّى فَقُل إِنِّى قَرِيبٌ
When my slaves ask you about me, TELL THEM, I am near. NO! He says..... وَإِذَا سَأَلَكَ عِبَادِى عَنِّى..... when my slaves ask you about me..... فَإِنِّى قَرِيبٌ..... I am near. Allah took out the prophet (Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam) from the middle, and inserted himself directly into the conversation. This is the amazing rhetoric of the Quran! How Allah actually displays his instant nearness right in the verse. SubhanAllah!
So when Allah is called upon, he's right there; at that very instant! And we know that angels flock around the gathering where the name of Allah is being mentioned. If only we had the eyes to witness it, we would see angels all around us right now; praying for us; asking forgiveness for us, just because we are mentioning the name of their beloved.

So the first step in acquiring this knowledge, is that we all display the proper ADAB for this glorious king of kings.  If possible, be in a state of wudu when you listen to these sessions, because
حصول العلم من العبادۃ
The acquisition of knowledge is a worship. And there is no worship without WUDU. Act just like when you're in the state of prayer. While you're playing, you don't check your emails or update your status on facebook (hopefully). It's the same thing here. We're all in a state of worship right now. Don't do anything that reduces or diminishes the sanctity of this worship. Be mindful of whose presence we're in right now. Allah(Subhanhu Wa Ta'ala) is bestowing his greatest gift upon all of us. Don't play on your phones while receiving this gift; don't text; don't email; don't do anything that would reduces or diminishes the sanctity of this worship. Because this really is life changing knowledge. May Allah open up the doors of our hearts to take in this divine knowledge, and purify our hearts with it.


10 Foundational Principles of knowledge

The ancient arabs used 10 criteria; or 10 measuring sticks; before they would attempt to learn something. They called it مَبادِی العَشرۃ (the 10 fundamental principle of knowledge). They believed that before one embarks on a journey of knowledge, one must know 10 things about that knowledge. In our terms today, this would translate to, "Do your homework!". So they had 10 fundamental criteria
 
أِنَّ مبادئ العلوم العشرة؛ الحدُّ و المَوضُوعُ ثُمَّ ثَمرۃ
و فَضلُهُ ونِسبَةُ و الوَاضِعُ و الاسمُ ؛ و الِاستِمدَادُ و الشَّارِع و الحُکمُ
وَ مَسائِلُ و البَعضُ بِالبَعضُ اِكتفى ؛ و مَن دَرَى الجِميعُ حَازْ الشُّرَفَا
 
Now let's first understand these criteria before we apply them to what we're about to study.

الحد

The first one is AL-HADD, which in the English language we call a "definition". It is important to know the definition of what we are studying. In the science of logic, a definition is something that gets to the essence of a thing. In other words when you define something, you have to define it "essentially". A proper definition should always be inclusive, as well as exclusive. It should include everything that that thing is; and it should exclude everything that the thing isn't. 
To give you an example of this, logicians say that a human being is a "rational animal". Human beings are animals, but they are rational animals. This is a definition that gets to the "essence" of what a human being is. To be human is to be specifically from the animal kingdom (and not from any other kingdom, such as the mineral or plant kingdom); but in addition to being an animal, it also has to be rational; it has to be sapiential; it must have intellect and the ability to reason; whereas other animals don't have that ability. So this definition excludes all the other kingdoms, and it also excludes the irrational animals that operate based on instinct. So the first thing we need to know is the definition of what you are about to learn.

و المَوضُوعُ

Then you have the MAWDOOAH, which is the subject matter. What is this thing that you are learning about? 
 

ثَمرَۃ

Then there is the SAMRAH, which is the fruit. What is the benefit of studying this thing? What is the end goal?
 

فضلہ

Then you have the FADAL; the virtue of that thing. Why should that thing be learned? Because not all knowledge should we learned. There are certain types of knowledge that are superfluous. There are others that are a complete waste of time, like commercial jingles, or songs. You can learn this stuff but it is a waste of time. And this is why in one of our prayers that we recite after FARD we say:
اللھم انی اسئلک علم نافعاً،
Oh Allah! I ask you for beneficial knowledge. I ask you for a knowledge that will benefit my heart; because there certainly is knowledge that can be detremental to the heart; that can destroy the heart. Most of us are filled with trivia! I personally would like those parts of my brain back that are occupied by that trivia. But unfortunately it is something that we are all stuck with. But what we are planning to study is not. This is an incredibly fruitful knowledge! So we must know its virtue; and we must understand why it is worthy our time, our effort and our consideration. 

نسبۃُ

And then knowing what it relates to. In other words, what other types of knowledge are associated with it? What are the companion fields of knowledge that link in with this one.
 

الواضِعُ

Who is the first one who made this science. Who is the source of this knowledge. And this is really important, because we don't want to invest time acquiring a certain knowledge, if we're not source about its source. Why spend all this time studying about something, only to find out that it came from an inauthentic source.

الأسمُ

What are its name or names? Because every knowledge has a name or it can have several names.

الاستِمدادُ

This is to know the basic principles based on which a particular knowledge is derived.

الشارع  و الحکم

And to understand the legal ruling of that knowledge. Is it الفرض عین , is it فرض الکِفایۃ ;  is it obligatory knowledge, or is it simply recommended? Is it mandatory knowledge or is it optional?

المسائِلُ

And then to know the intricate details of the subject. In other words, to study the topics that make up the body of that knowledge.
 
Then he says:
و مَن دَرَى الجِميعُ حَازْ الشُّرَفَا
The one who understands these 10 fundamentals of knowledge, he attains the most honorable rank in that knowledge. So these are what the ancient Arabs called مَبادِی العَشرۃ

10 Fundamental Principles of TAWHEED

Hadd
In terms of Quran, it is defined as the word of Allah(Subhanhu Wa Ta'ala). It deals with the subject of recognizing him. It deals with the nature of his RUBUBIYYAH. It deals with the nature of his ILAHYIYYAH; with the nature of ASMA WA SIFAAT (his names and attributes). So Quran is the study of who Allah(Subhanhu Wa Ta'ala) is. And then it is a study of what he wants from his creation. It is a study of the purpose of creation. It deals with the nature of his prophets and their WALAYAH (the guardianship). It deals with the subject of his kingship and his lordship over the day of judgment, and then the resulting rewards and punishment.

MAWDOOAH 

The subject matter of Quran is TAQWA. It was sent down by Allah(Subhanhu Wa Ta'ala) through this beloved messenger to human beings (الانسان), in order for them to achieve a state of TAQWA - which is science of mindfulness of their creator. That is the subject of the Quran. For man to come to know who his creator is, and what he wants.

SAMRAH

The fruit, or the the benefit of this knowledge is that it prevents us, or protects us from making mistakes in our understanding of Allah(Subhanhu Wa Ta'ala). For example there are people that think that the universe is eternal. It's always been there. Quran is very clear that ALLAH is
بَدِیعُ السموات و الارض
who originated the heavens and the earth without any prior pattern. That is what BADEE means; an originator who brings something into the world without any prior pattern
علی غیر مثال المُسبَق
and then he is also فاطر السموات و الارض, the one who brought forth the heavens and the earth. He is also خالق السموات و الارض , the creator of the earth. He is ALBAREE, He is ALMUSAWWIR.
So if we just look at the ACT of creation, so many names and attributes emerge from this one act. He is BADEE because he did not use a prior pattern for his design; he is KHALIQ for physically creating the universe; he is FAATIR because the act of his creation was explosive and instantaneous in nature; He is BAREE because he gave a material aspect to all creation; he is MUSAWWIR, because he fashioned, and gave everything a particular form and shape. So we have so many different names, just to understand his one single act of creation.

So you can see that this knowledge is very important to learn; because we have theory, and we have practice. When we understand the true essence of the theory, it changes the way we practice it. When we learn that Allah is just, and that it is inconceivable for God to be unjust; then all matters of QADA and QADR (destiny and will) become easier to understand and accept. When we understand he is MALIK and we are MAMLOOK; when we understand he is RABB and we are MARBOOB (we are literall his possessions); it changes the way in which we look at trials and tribulations. 

Allah can give us a wonderful life or he could give us a miserable life. He can do whatever he wants. That is the Allah that is transcendent! That is the God that is frightening! Who is the ZUL JALAAL! He is the God of tsunamis; he is the God of holocausts; He is the God of fire; He is the God that allows cancer; he is the God that takes little children away from their parents. These are all aspects of his transcendence! of his JALAAL. And as his creation and slaves we are in submission to these aspect of his will, to the best of our abilities, whether we like it or not; because we understand, that one, we have no power and no ability to counter his will; and two, that he would not be unjust with us. So if we are in an undesirable state, there is divine wisdom behind it, which we need to learn and understand.

But then he is not only ZUL JALAAL. He is ZUL JALAALI - WAL IKRAAM.  He is also the God of immense blessing;  he is the God of overflowing bounties; he is the God who gave the gift of life; who gave us the gift of sustenance; He is the God who manifests in the unconditional love of a mother; the affections of a sister; the comforts of a true friend.  He is the god who eases the heart of the grieving mother who lost her child; he is the God who cures cancer when all doctors give up on the patient; he is the God who brings the dead earth back to life; he is the God who hides his bounties in his terrors; and He is the God who hides his blessings in his tribulations.

So learning all these things helps us deal with the vicissitudes of time. It helps us deal with the challenges and the calamities of our lives. And this is the fruit of this knowledge.

And then studying the nature of his prophets and his loved ones. Finding out for instance, that the prophets have the greatest trials of all. Because nobody who has ever lost a child, can feel anything but consolation, when they learn that the prophet (Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam) burried all of his sons. So the very best of creation had all of his children taken from him, and yet he never complained. Even though he did weep. His eyes teared up when he lost his children. We know that because its in the HADEES. But he never complained. And he said, Allah does not take you to account for your eyes. In other words, he does not take you to account for your grief; but he takes you to account for what you say in that state of grief. 

So that's one of the greatest benefits of learning this theory, and then beginning to exercise this theory in the laboratory of our lives. Because this knowledge is really about being out there in the world. Religion is essentially an experience. And this is why there is no knowledge without practice, and there's no practice without knowledge. We have to first gain this knowledge, and then bring it to life, by putting it into practice, by worshipping our creator. This knowledge of our DEEN, is not simply a theoretical knowledge for us. It is a very practical knowledge. We this knowledge we stand before him in prayer; we call out to him; and as we call upon him, he comes closer to us. And when he comes closer, our souls are able to take that imprint, of his divine presence. 

So the fruit and the end goal of this knowledge is to understand our creator; and to not make errors and mistakes in our understanding of our creator. This knowledge of Quran protects us. Quran is like a chaperone! And even though lovers don't like chaperones, because they always get in the way; but a chaperone is important to prevent anything illegitimate happening between lovers. So in our love affair with Allah(Subhanhu Wa Ta'ala), this Quran becomes our chaperone. It's there so we don't say or think anything that is incorrect, or inappropriate for our beloved.

FADAL

The virtue of this knowledge is that the knowledge of Allah is the highest of all knowledge!
التوحید أجل العلم
Understanding the unity of Allah(Subhanhu Wa Ta'ala) is the most esteemed of all knowledge. Because it has to do with the relationship of the creation with their creator. And this realationship is the greatest gift that we can receive; therefore it has the highest virtue.

NISBAH

Its NISBA, its association, is to all of the علوم الشَّرَعِیَّۃ, all the sciences of Islamic law. Its related to DAYAM AL-ISLAM - the seven pillars and practices of Islam. In the light of this knowledge we practice ISLAM through WALAYAH, TAHARAH, SALAAT, ZAKAAT, SAUM, HAJJ and JEHAD

WAADI

The وَاضِع, the one who formulated this knowledge first and foremost is Allah(Subhanhu Wa Ta'ala), through the revelation of Quran. Because Quran is a revelation that tells us about the nature of God. It tells us that: 
فعلم انہ لا الہ الا اللہ
Know! that there is no God but Allah. Allah(Subhanhu Wa Ta'ala) tells us QUL HUWAL LLAHU AHAD. Know that god is unique. He tells us that he is the creator of the heavens and the earth.  He tells us that he is QAAHIRUN FAWQA IBADIHI (He possesses complete and overwhelming control of his slaves). He says he is ALHAYYE ALQAYYUM - the ever living and the sustainer of the heavens and earth. So much of the Quranic narrative is about who Allah is.
But then it is also the prophet (Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam) that brough this knowledge to us. Allah gave us the Quran through the medium of his beloved messenger (Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam). And then also to know, that this divine message was absorbed, internalized and then simplified by his WASEE (Ali Ibn Abu Talib), who then gave it to the Imams, who then gave it to the DUAAT, until it was simplified to the point where common people like us could access it through its various form of SHAREEAH, TAVEEL, MABDA MAAD and HAQAIQ.

ISM

This knowledge is named ZIKR AL-HAKEEM (the wise reminder). From this knowledge we also get the term: USOOL AdDEEN, (the roots of religion). Because what this religion is built upon essentially, is TAWHEED. And Quran explains to us the science of TAWHEED.

HUKM SHARI

The ruling on its acquisition is that it is FARD AIN. It is obligatory for every MUSLIM to know their creator. Because Rasul Allah (Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam) said in a HADEES
طلب العلم فریضۃ علی کل مسلم و مسلمۃ
The acquisition of knowledge is FARD (obligatory) on every Muslim man and woman. Someone once asked Hazrat Abdullah that is acquiring knowledge greater or praying SUNNAT and NAFAL prayers greater? That's when he quoted this HADEES and said that Rasul Allah (Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam) called learning of knowledge a FARD; and FARD is always greater than SUNNAH and NAAFIL

MASAIL

The details of this science are all the topics that InshaAllah we will cover in the sessions. 

So these are the MABADEE ASHARA; the 10 fundamental principles; the 10 primary reasons of why we should study the Quran.








 
Hadees of Rasul Allah(Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam)
مَن عَرَفَ نَفسَہُ فَقَدْ عَرَفَ رَبُّہُ
The one who knows himself, comes to know his lord. In other words, self-awareness is directly proportional to the awareness of one's creator. So let's begin getting to know our master, by getting to know ourselves.

The human body is an extraordinary creation of Allah(Subhanhu Wa Ta'ala). If you have any knowledge about the human anatomy, you know that each of organ of the human body has a very specific job. The heart's job is to pump and circulate blood to the entire body. No other organ does that job. It is is unique in that particular function. The brain's job is to process and control the actions of the body - it deals with memory, imagination, recollection. Liver helps in digestion, among other things; kidneys keep the blood clean from toxins. In other words, every internal organ of the body has its own unique function. Similary if you look at the external organs, the eyes have the task of sight; the ears have the job of hearing; tounge can taste; nose can smell. All these organs (internal or external),  they all have very distinct and unique task assigned to them - and they do not share their job responsibilities with each other. The eyes will never hear; the nose will never taste; the liver will never pump blood.

So studying the human body, we see that Allah(Subhanahu wa Ta'ala) created each organ with its own unique and specific task, that is functionally disconnected from the tasks of other organs. But what's really interesting, is that even though each organ is disconnected from the other in its job and function, they are all working together to accomplish one single goal, which is to keep the human body alive and operational. So the human body is a perfect exhibition of "unity through diversity".

To control and operate this human body, Allah(Subhanhu Wa Ta'ala) created the RUH (Soul/Spirit). So the human body is like a car - the organs are like the various components that make the car operational - like the engine, the shocks, windshield, wheels, braking system etc. The RUH is the driver who is operating the car - controlling its motion, steering it in the right or wrong direction. Without a driver, even the most expensive lambergini is nothing more than a chunk of metal.  In other words, what truly gives value to a car; what truly accomplishes the purpose for which the car was made, is the driver. Similarly what truly gives meaning and purpose to the body, is the spirit or the RUH. And just like human begins existed before cars were invented, the RUH was created before the body. There is a HADEES of Rasul Allah(Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam) in which he says that Allah(Subhanhu Wa Ta'ala) created the RUH 2000 years before he created us in the physical world. So the creation of spirit *preceeds* the creation of the body. And even though the RUH is one single entity, just like the body, it is also made up of various different parts; each with its own unique job description.

So first we'll look at these components from a bird's eye view, and then we'll look at them in detail. Right now I just want you to differentiate these components in your mind by their function. And understand that this is the Quranic perspective. I don't know to what extent modern psychology would agree with this - but I'd love to hear those perspectives as well, and see if there is a way we can reconcile those differences in understanding in the light of Quran. Nevertheless, what I'm sharing with you today, is how the Quran explains the human spirit - or the RUH.

Today we know that a fetus becomes alive in the 4th month in its mother's womb. Quran defines 7 stages of embryonic development:
وَلَقَدْ خَلَقْنَا ٱلْإِنسَنَ مِن سُلَلَةٍۢ مِّن طِينٍۢ
And indeed We created man out of an extract of clay.
ثُمَّ جَعَلْنَهُ نُطْفَةًۭ فِى قَرَارٍۢ مَّكِينٍۢ
Thereafter We made him as a Nutfah in a safe lodging.
ثُمَّ خَلَقْنَا ٱلنُّطْفَةَ عَلَقَةًۭ فَخَلَقْنَا ٱلْعَلَقَةَ مُضْغَةًۭ فَخَلَقْنَا ٱلْمُضْغَةَ عِظَمًۭا فَكَسَوْنَا ٱلْعِظَمَ لَحْمًۭا ثُمَّ أَنشَأْنَهُ خَلْقًا آخَرَ ۚ فَتَبَارَكَ ٱللَّهُ أَحْسَنُ ٱلْخَلِقِينَ
Then We made the drop of sperm into a clinging clot, and We made the clot into a lump, and We made from the lump bones, and We covered the bones with flesh; then we developed him into his final form. So blessed is Allah the best of creators.



RUH
So the quran describes the development of the body (جسم) in 7 stages distinct stages. The first three stages - نطفۃ، علقۃ، مضغۃ, take 40 days each to complete. So at the completion of 120 days, which is 4 months, Allat activates the machinery of that body by connecting the RUH with it.  And what is this RUH? It is that same primordeal energy that Allah(Subhanahu wa Ta'ala) created in the spiritual realm. Once the child comes out of its mothers womb into the world, and passes through its initial stages of infancy, you can see the RUH slowing taking control of the body. The infant begins to move its eyes, then starts rolling over. It then gains control of its arms and legs - it starts crawling; then starts attempting to stand up on two feet; then gradually begins walking. These are all stages of the RUH gradually taking control of the body.

NFS

The next component that developes in the child, is the NAFS. Modern psychology calls this "self" or the "ego". It's also referred to in Arabic as أنا, which means "me". When I use the word "I", or "me" in a sentence, I'm actually referring to a particular component of my RUH, which is my NAFS - the soul! So you can see, there is a very clear distinction in the Arabic language between the spirit and the soul. The spirit (RUH) is the original, pure, primordeal energy that what was created in the spiritual realm; whereas the soul (the NAFS) is a component of the spirit, that develops in the earlier years of childhood. So the infant, who just took control of his body, now starts becoming conscious and aware of his own individual existence. He no longer views himself as his body. The perception changes from "I am my body" to "I HAVE a body". In other words, the driver is now beginning to drive the car. And this is why the child learns to say "NO". Before that, the child is all "YES Sirreee". Bring me a glass of water - YES SIR! Go punch your sister! YES SIR! Jump into the well! YES SIR. But then as the NAFS develops, it learns to say NO. It begins to assert its own "identity", and declare its own presence in time and space. I am my own person! I am not just an extension of my parents, I actually have my own existential presence. And so now things turn into MY toy! "I" won't eat this, "I" don't like that, I WILL do that. I want that toy! Parents will generally look at this behaviour as disobedience - but from the child's perspective, he's literally wiring himself up to see what happens when I say "NO"?  It's all part of the childs spiritual development process. So with the development of this component (the NAFS), the driver has now entered the car.


According to the Islamic tradition, NAFS is the one in-charge of the body, because it's closest to the body. In some advanced religious texts, it has been explained that the NAFS is wearing the body like a glove. This is why the effects of the soul can be experienced through the body.


AQL
As the child continues to grow, based on the information he takes in from his parent, family, school, his friends, his environment, a third component of the RUH begins to develop which is generally called العقل (The intellect). In general terms this is also referred to as the "mind" or the "intellect". The Quran however refers to this particular component as the قلب (The heart).

This is one of the most misunderstood term in the context of religion. Often times the heart and the Intellect (قلب and عقل) get confused with the physical heart and the physical brain. Because the physical heart is also called QALB and the reasoning center of the physical brain - is also called AQL. So when most people hear the word QLB, they think of the physical heart; and when they hear the word AQL, they think of the physical brain. But in the Quranic or religious context, QLB does not refer to the physical heart and AQL does not refer to the physical brain. In the Quranic context, both AQL and QLB refer to the heart and the intellect of the RUH (the spirit). So just as the physical body has a heart and an intellect, the spirit or the RUH also has a heart and the intellect. And it is this pair that is being addressed everywhere in the Quran.



To keep thing simple, Im going to refer to this component as the "MIND". Now the mind is made up of two parts. The first is the spiritual QLB (the spiritual heart); and the second is the spiritual AQL (the spiritual "intellect"). These together form the command center - the think tank. Whenever I want to do something - or according to our new terminology, whenever my NAFS wants to do something, both the heart and the intellect will inevitibly come into play. So I want to put my face in the fire - because it looks so pretty! Who wants to put the face in the fire? The NAFS. Where is that desire to do that genius act coming from? from the heart! That desire, that IRADAH will then be presented to the AQL, which will say... NO! What are you an idiot? You'll burn your face! Don't do it. So you see, anytime the NAFS wants to do something, the spiritual heart and the spiritual intellect will automatically get engaged. This is why these components are always referred together as a single unit. Now why does the Quran call this pair, QALB? Why does the term QALB superseed the term AQL? Because the spiritual heart (QALB) is the ultimate king. Eventually what the spiritual heart dictates, the NAFS will obey. So my AQL can tell me that I'll burn my face if I put it into the fire, but if my desire to do that act is overwhelming, it will overrule the advice of the intellect, and my NAFS, or "I" will put my face in the fire. You understand? So AQL is great; it's powerful! But in the end the NAFS will do what the heart commands. And we have all experienced this in our daily lives. We all know that when its time for prayers, we must drop whatever we're doing, and stand before Allah in prayer. We all know that! Our AQL (spiritual intellect) tells us that. But the heart wants to finish the episode of Vampire Diaries; the heart wants to watch that movie; it wants to play tennis or basketball. And so what ends up happening? The spiritual hearts desire "overrules" the recommendations of the spiritual intellect, and the NAFS ends up fulfilling that desire. This is why the Quran refers to this component as the QLB - because it is the overriding rubrick under which AQL gets subsumed.

So as the child continues to grow, his NAFS keeps collecting information from his nurturing environment, parents, teachers, friends, and based on what it learns, this third component of the spiritual heart and the spiritual intellect develop; and this process continues until the child reaches maturity. At this point the child has now grown into an adult. His NAFS now has full control, of not just his body, but also his spiritual heart and his spiritual intellect. The RUH has developed the ability to think; to feel; to reason; to differentitate between right and wrong based on whatever nurturing that was provided to him by his environment.

Linguistic look at terms.







So the first component is the RUH itself which Allah(Subhanahu wa Ta'ala)'s primorideal creation. This is the perfect spirit that was created in the spiritual realm.


 
 


The third component is NAFS.


The fourth component is JISM, which is the physical component of our existence.

Now if you look at this diagram, if we move from the outer realms of RUH (Spirit) towards the JISM (body), you'll notice there's a certain density and mass that begins to develop. In other words, we can see the RUH, journey from pure spirituality to pure physicality.

The RUH itself is a pure and subtle energy (LATEEF). It is so subtle that we are not even aware of its existence.
The mind or the intelligence (QLB and AQL) come next which is a bit more dense. It is not as subtle as the RUH. It's existence begins to get noticed. This is where self-awareness generates, with the development of the mind. It gives us a state of consciousness where we can "see" or "witness" ourselves in action. We can actually think about what we're thinking. No other creature on the planet besides human beings can do that.
The next is NAFS, which is more dense and heavier than the mind, and it is the closest component to (neighbours) the physical body. Scholares have described this relationship between the NAFS and the body, as the NAFS، wearing the body like a glove. This is why, in psycho-therapy,  there is so much importance placed on feeling sensations in the body. This is also what they refer to as memory of the body. If you close your eyes right now and imagine the last time you went out with a very close friend, or a loved one, and had a really amazing time, you'll notice that you'll begin feeling certain sensations in your body. Maybe you'll get butterflies in your stomach, or maybe your heart will begin to race with excitement. Why is that? You're not with them right now! So why are you feeling those sensations? Because the NAFS is powerful enough to relive those moments through memory, and generate the sensations in the body again.
This is also why the NAFS has such a strong affinity towards physicality; because it is closely aligned with the nature of physicality. NAFS is where desires and temptations generate. And what is the common denominator for all desires and temptations? Physicality! Material posessions! Worldly fame! Rank! Status! Money! Everything related to DUNIYA (this physical and material world). And then there is the JISM, which is complete physicality. In other words, JISM is the degraded or dense form of the spirit, where pure energy has been reduced to mass. Vapor has turned into a block of ice. And this is why in our Islamic theology, we don't look at the two as separate disconnected entities. Rather we see them as very connected entities, with one component having a marked influence on the other. And this is why if you study the SHAREEAH, you'll find that each and every aspect of it takes both into consideration. You'll never find a physical command that does not have a spiritual relevance, and you'll never find a spiritual command, that does not have a physical action related to it.

If we look at this diagram from the inside out. The innermost component is the physical body itself. Then as we move towards the RUH, the next component is NAFS, which is the lowest principle of man. It is the lowest level of spirituality. Higher than the NAFS is the rank of the mind (Qalb and AQL); and higher than the mind is the rank of the RUH (spirit). So as we move away from the body towards the spirit, things get more and more subtle and pure; and as we move from the sprity towards the body, things get more dense and heavy.

Imam Ja'far al-Sadiq (AS) describes the NAFS as the ZALIM (the tyrant). He describes the QLB as the MUQTASID (the moderate). And the RUH  as SABIQ (preceding one, winner). He says the ZALIM (NAFS) is the one that loves God for his own sake. It is a selfish kind of love. It is loving God to take advantage of God, in this life and the afterlife. The MUQTASID (mind) is the one who loves God for God. He gains knowledge about Allah(Subhanhu Wa Ta'ala) and comes to love him, for his beauty and his majesty. The third one, the SQBIQ, is the one who annihilates his own will for the will of God. He only wishes what Allah wishes. He only desires what Allah desires. In other words, he annihilates his own existence within the existence of his lord.

 
RUH (Spirit)
 
This is the component that sees Allah. This is the non-individual aspect of the soul. This is "true being" ALWUJOOD. RUH is used for angels who saw the soul within ADAM whereas IBLESS being NAFS only saw clay.

RUH is purely metaphysical that has absolutely no affinity with the physical

 
Finally the ruh or spirit is an entity not well defined or described by Islamic source texts. The Quran responds in answer to the question about the ruh that it is a command from God.
“And they ask you, [O Muhammad], about the soul. Say, ‘The soul is of the affair of my Lord.And mankind have not been given of knowledge except a little.’”  (Quran, 17:85, SaheehInternational, trans.)
Little is known about the soul with Ghazali (2007, p. 8) explaining that “most intelligences(uqul) and understandings (afham) are unable to grasp” its true nature. As to whether the nafs (ego) and ruh  (spirit) refer to the same or different entities, several theories exist with the scholar Ibn Abi Al-Izz for example asserting that the two terms refer to the same metaphysical entity that however takes on a different status in relation to the human body. When it is housed within the physical body, the nafs

The spirit ruh is in direct connection with the Divine, even if one is unconscious of that connection. The spirit has seven levels or facets of the complete spirit. These levels are: mineral, vegetable, animal, personal, human, secret and secret of secret souls. Each level represents the stages of evolution, and the process that it goes through in its growth. The spirit is holistic, and extends to all aspects of the person, i.e. the body, the mind and the soul. Each level of the spirit has valuable gifts and strengths, as well as weaknesses. The goal is to develop the strengths and to achieve a balance between these levels, not forgoing the lower ones to focus only on the higher ones. In traditional psychology, Ego psychology deals with the animal soul, Behavioral psychology focuses on the conditioned functioning of the vegetable and animal soul, Cognitive psychology deals with the mental functions of the personal soul, Humanistic psychology deals with the activities of the human soul and Transpersonal psychology deals with ego-transcending consciousness of the secret soul and the secret of secret souls.

Arabic poetry ruh is used to mean breath and wind, while nafs connotes the self or person but relates to breath and wind too.
ruh is extremely subtle




So Knowledge begins with ADAB, and the ultimate purpose of all divine knowledge is the purification of the heart. There is an entire branch of philosophy called TASAWWFU, that deals with this science. And all of its teachings come directly from the sayings of Maulana Ali (AS) in Nehjul Balagha and other records. All SUFIS unanimously accept of Maulana Ali (AS) as their leader. Unfortunately much like ADAB, this topic is also probably the most forgotten, amongst all of the other teachings of Islam.
The idea here, is that before you study knowledge you have to purify your heart. Because you will never benefit from knowledge if your heart is impure. The heart is the most prized posessession of a believer. That's why Quran teaches us DUAS like:
رَبَّنَا لَا تُزِغْ قُلُوبَنَا بَعْدَ إِذْ هَدَيْتَنَا
Don't cause our hearts to deviate, after you've guided us to this pure knowledge. There are many words in the Quran that Allah uses to express his wrath for the disbelievers; and that wrath almost always manifests by affecting their hearts.
ختم اللہ علی قلوبھم, Allah seals their hearts. HAJABA, QASWA..... فَقَسَتْ قُلُوبُهُمْ..... their hearts become hard.
كَلَّا..... بَلْۜ رَانَ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِهِم
their hearts become encrusted.
فَلَمَّا زَاغُوٓا۟ أَزَاغَ ٱللَّهُ قُلُوبَهُمْ
When they deviated, Allah caused their hearts to deviate. There are many many words..... أَکِنَّ, their hearts are locked; أقفَلَ,
أَفَلَا يَتَدَبَّرُونَ ٱلْقُرْآنَ أَمْ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبٍ أَقْفَالُهَآ
Why don't they reflect on the Quran, are their hearts locked? These are all words to show that all destruction begins with the heart.
 
So for a believer his/her heart is the most important entity; and what purifies and cleanses the heart is knowledge. But the heart needs to be prepared for knowledge. It is like preparing the earth for planting. Before you actually puts seed into the earth, you have to cultivate the earth with a plow. And that is exactly what one needs to do with the heart. The heart has to plowed in order to remove the negative qualities, before it can be adorned with the positive qualities. 
اول التَّواضَع التَّکَلُّف
The beginning of humility is forcing oneself to be humble. And so the philosophy of TASAWWUF, is completely focused on this aspect of humility. Its root word SAWF means "wool". So TASAWWUF literally means to "wear wool". Some of the early people used to wear wool, as a type of declaration of their position in the world. Wool is not very comfortable; it is kind of rough. So the idea of the Sufisim, is to deal with this world by literally giving it up. This does not mean abandoning family and going into exile. What it means is to give up the fleeting pleasures and the vain desires of this world for a greater purpose. To exist in the world, free of all attachments. And if you study the SEERAH of Rasul Allah and Maulana Ali (AS), this is exactly what you will find. The willingly accepted, and submitted to the discomforts of this world for a greater cause. In other words, they gave up the beautiful silky and shiny garments of this physical world, and put on this garment of wool. And that is the idea of TASAWWFU. We'll talk a lot more about this when we study the topic of Ehsaan InshaAllah.

We're going to look at QALB and AQL together.
اِذا اجْتَمَعَت أِفتَرَقَت..... واِذا افْتَرَقَت أِجتَمَعَت
When these terms occur together in a single context, they separate out in meaning; and each word takes on its own particular meaning. But if they are used in isolation, then one term takes on the meanings of the other terms. In other words, the outer term bring all of the inner meanings together.


QALB

The word قلب linguistically means to turn something around on its face; to flip something over, to invert something, like turning over a cloth. So whatever side was on top, goes to the bottom. If a person is walking in one direction and then he turns away from that direction and starts walking in the opposite direction, that's called قَلَبَ  . Arabs would say قَلَبَ کلاماً  he flipped the conversation. Meaning, he was talking about one thing, and he switched to a different subject. The word انقلاب comes from the same root word, which means "a revolution". The idea of every revolt, is to turn things around.

Now why is the heart called قلب ? Because it never rests. It is constant flipping between two states - expansion and contraction. And what does the heart do? It nourishes the entire body. It is constantly working to pump and deliver oxygenated blood to all parts of the body. Now the idea behind the spiritual heart is that it is doing exactly the same thing. The spiritual heart is constantly working to nourish the soul. It's pumping and delivering wisdom and guidance to the soul. It is constantly working to purify the soul of its evil personality traits and it to keep it healthy and alive. There is a HADEES in which Rasul Allah(Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam) says:
ألا وَ اِنَّ فِی الجَسَد مُضغَۃً۔ وَأِذَا صَلَحَت صَلَحَ الْجَسَدُ کُلُّہُ؛  وَأِذَا فَسَدَتْ فَسَدَ الْجَسَدُ کُلُّہُ۔ اَلا وَ ھِیَ القَلْبُ
Beware! Indeed within the body of a human being, there is a lump of flesh. When it is sound, the entire body is sound; and when it is corrupt the entire body is corrupt. We know this statement is absolutely true in the physical sense. Healthy heart - healthy mind and healthy body. If the heart is weak, the functions of the mind and the body deterirorate proportionally. But it also holds true for the spiritual heart. If the knowledge and the intentions that are being pumped into the soul are pure and accurate, it will manifest as good actions in the world. But if the knowledge and intentions are incorrect, it will result in evil and corruption in the world.

Another beautiful image we get from the word قلب is to "turn back" to or "re-turn". To return to its original source. Because the heart was designed to RE-TURN to Allah. He was created in this world (DUNIYA) that is far away from his world, and so the idea of the QALB is to return back to Allah.
فَانْقَلَبُوا بِنِعْمَةٍ مِنَ اللَّهِ وَفَضْلٍ
So they returned to the blessings of Allah.

So everything we're going to talk about in these sessions will be related to this spiritual heart. This spiritual heart has nothing to do with the physical organ.
It is this spiritual heart that contains the deeper intelligence and wisdom. It holds the Divine spark or spirit and is the place of gnosis and deep spiritual knowledge. In Sufism, the goal is to develop a heart that is sincere, loving and compassionate, and to develop the heart's intelligence, which is deeper, and more grounded than the rational, abstract intelligence of the mind.
According to Sufic psychology emotions are from the self or nafs, not from the heart. The qalb mediates between the Nafs and spirit. Its task is control the nafs and direct the man toward the spirit.

Is the center of will (IRADAH). The word means "to change". This is one single organ of the body that never rests. Every other organ get some sort of relief during the day . Even the brain shuts down for a few hours when we sleep. This is when we have dreamless sleep; but then it turns on again and we begin dreaming. Heart is the only single organ in the body that has absolutely no rest - not for a moment. The only time it comes to rest is at the time of death.

The heart is the king of the body.


So that's qalb literally, linguistically, physically and spiritually... Now metaphorically قلب was also used by the Arabs in the meaning of a vessel or a container... Hmmm where did we hear that word container before? Intellect! So not just the word عقل but the word قلب is also used to represent a container..... Maulana Ali once held Kumail Ibn Ziayad's hand and took him to a cemetrary and said to him...
يَا كُمَيْل بْن زِيَادٍ، إِنَّ هذهِ الْقُلُوبَ أَوْعِيَةٌ فَخَيْرُهَا أَوْعَاهَا
O Kumayl, these hearts are containers. And the best container is that which preserves its contents...
Finally قلب is also used for the essence, or the innermost part of something...

So when Allah created us in this physical realm. and put his divine attributes in ALL of his creation . outside us AND inside us. فی الافاق وفی الانفس .  He gave us a gift. He gave us something very special . that could help us recognize these attributes. and that could help us connect back with Him. And that is the heart. He gave us  a heart. a qalb. which a medium to see and recognize his divine glory. He gave us this spiritual heart. and he made it the center of our consciousness. so that we can recoginize his attributes. his signs. his logo in everything around us.

And he made the physical heart. a symbol for that spiritual heart.

Just look at the physical heart. It is THE most astonishing organ of the body. We now know. that the human heart is pulsating approximately 40 million times a year. In an average human lifespan.  your heart will pulsate about 3 billion times. It takes about 40 gallons of blood in our. If you get 40 buckets. Not 40 gallons. Just 40 buckets.  and you fill them with water. and start moving those buckets from one room to another. You'll see how quickly you get tired. right? . And yet this extraordinary organ that Allah has put into our bodies. it continues to pump throughout our lives.  effortlessly. Not asking us for ANYTHING in return. Not even asking us to tell it to pump this blood for us.

And what's even more unusual is that we now know that the human heart. is not only self-regulating. it begins beating before there is a central nervous system. During embryonic development, the heart is one of the few organs that must function almost as soon as it is formed. And the fact.  that the heart is one of the earliest organs to develop speaks.  to its importance. The human heart begins to beat and pump blood through the embryo around the 22nd day of pregnancy. Who made the heart beat before there was even a central nervous system? Who made the heartbeat? The one who created the heart made it beat. And sustain its beat. Why? . Because it is the heart that has been created to recognize Allah. That's why it is the first thing that develops to symbolize the human need.  of understanding its maker. And it keeps doing its job until one day it stops beating. And then this form called the soul. Rooh.  leaves this physical realm and goes into a different realm.

And this heart. this Qalb. Qalb is spelled. Qaf laam and ba. Now if you take those letters and you switch them around. You get the word qabal. which means to come before. To preceed. To be the first and foremost.  And there is this science in the Arabic language . that when you switch the letters around. For example you have a word like علم . Which means knowledge. When you switch the same letters around you get the word عمل . Which means action. Because knowledge is related to action. So when the letters of a word are switched around to form a different word. they're always connected in meaning. This is another miraculous aspect of the Arabic lanuage. And that's why Qalb and Qabl. heart and to preceed . or to come before are related. And then the word Qabl and Qibla are also related.And Qibla means direction. And a direction is always pointed towards a destination. If there is no destination. there is no need for directions. So then.  what is the direction of the heart?.  Who is the destination of the heart? . It's the one it learnt about . BEFORE it learnt anything else? Who is this heart yearning for? Who is it pointing to. Who is it taking precedence for? Its doing it for Allah! . Because the heart knows something. that it knew even BEFORE it came into this realm. You see the heart knows.Alastu be rabbikum. Am I not your lord?

The heart knows the answer to that question. Am I not your master? Bala shahidna. Indeed we testify that you are . So every human heart that came into this world knows this truth. this spiritual truth. I bet you're feeling it right now. because everything that I'm saying . you already know that its the truth .

So we were made a witness in the spiritual realm. and now in this physical realm the . human being has been given the task of testifying again. by recognizing the divine attributes of his master within the horizons and his soul. He is tasked with repeating his testimony. the same testimony that he gave in the primordeal world. with Allah AWJ. He has to now testify that same thing in this physical world. with Sidq. صدقاً من قلبہ .  testify the truth. with sincerity of heart.

And what does he need to testify? What is it. that the heart needs to see. understand. believe and then testify? What is that? It is لا الہ الا اللہ . It has to testify that there is no Ilah. except Allah. he needs to purfiy the uniqueness and absoluteness. the ahadiyat and samadiyat. of Allah. from all His creation. He needs to purify his faith. Purify with what? . With his heart. and what is the human heart constantly doing. even in the physical realm? . It is purifying blood. SubhanAlah! . It is constantly working to exchange the deoxygenated blood . with the oxygenated blood. Oxygen is life. Eman . faith is spiritual life. It is constantly exchange kufr . for Emaan. and then supplying that oxygenated blood to the various parts of the body.

And so the human heart has been made a symbol for the spiritual heart.  Why has He made it a symbol? Because Allah (SUBHANHU WA TA'ALA) is manifesting himself from an unseen realm. You see . in the spiritual realm Allah was manifest. he was Zaahir. But in this physical realm he is Baatin. He is hidden. Allah has hidden himself from us. by manifesting himself. through his attributes.  in creation . from an unseen realm. And it is only the human heart. the spiritual heart that can recognize this.And this is why the heart is the organ of cognition. It's not the brain.

Now why does the heart beat? . Why does the heart beat? . Why does it go from contraction to expansion? . You see. Diastolic. Systolic. Why does it do that? . There are lots of ways you could get the blood around to the different parts of the body . without a beat. Why did Allah make it a beat? . And then why did Allah make the breadth. expansion and contraction?

Doctors can't answer that. Because medical science can explain the how. How does the heart beat?. medical science can explain that. Why does it beat?. Why isn't there a different mechanism to pump the blood. THAT science cannot explain.  But the Quran can!. The heart beats . it expands and contracts. because one of the attributes of Allah is AlBaasit. he is the expander. He is AlBasit and he is Al-Qabid. He is the Expander and the contractor. And that's what the heart is constantly doing. It is constantly contracting and expanding. Why? Because it needs to testify. It needs to testify that there is no Ilah except Allah. That's La Ilaha . Illa Allah.

La Ilaha. that's the contraction. That's negation of all Ilah. narrowing down all forms of false deities. contracting the space. removing and squeezing out every drop of deoxygenated blood.  until there is no Ilah left in our lives. That is contraction. That is La Ilaha . .  And then there is Illa Allah. filling it back with the life giving oxygen of Emaan. of faith. Recognizing and affirming Allah . THROUGH his divine attributes. and testifiying that there is ONLY Allah.  That's expansion . That's the oxygenated blood being pushed into every tissue . every muscle of our spiritual deeds. Our salaah. our fasts. our zakaat . our hajj.  The spiritual heart is constantly beating to the tune of La Ilaha. Illa Allah. It is contracting and expanding. to testify a message that is made up of contraction and expansion.

La Ila Illa Allah. is the beat of our spiritual hearts. It is what's constantly removing shirk and kufr from our actions. and replacing it with the life giving oxygen of faith. And its a continuous and constant process of purification. just as the heart is constantly and continuously engaged in the purification of the blood. Once the hearts stops pumping. what happens to the body? . It dies. What happens to the spiritual heart. that stops purifying itself from shirk and kufr?. It dies.

And now look at how the word Allah is pronounced. When you hear the heart through a stethoscope. Doctors say. it's lubbadubb. right? . They say it's making that lubb a dubb. sound. But when one of the Imams was asked. I hear my heart beating and making a sound. What is my heart saying? . The imam replied. It's saying.  Allah. Allah. Now if you actually listen to the sound. you don't hear the word Allah. right?.  So then what did the Imam mean? . This is not the word of an ordinary man. these are the words of an Imam.
کلام الامیر. امیر الکلام
speech of a king. and the king of all speech.

So what does it mean that the heart is saying Allah?. If you look at how this name Allah is pronounced. It is a also a contraction and an expansion. That's what it is. That's what the tongue is doing. All-lah! All-laah. That's what its doing. Say the word Allah right now. and see what happens when you pronounce the laam? You have to PRESS your tongue against top of your mouth. That's contraction. That's La Ilaha.  and then you need to expand the tongue.  bring it down. opening your mouth. to complete it. That's expansion. That's Illa Allah.  The name Allah is contraction and expansion. La Ilaha Illah Allah is contraction and expansion. In both contraction is first. expansion is second.

The heart is constantly contracting and expanding. calling out to its master. Allah. because the heart remembers its master. But the nafs. the soul. has forgotten who its master is. And this is because. we have become tainted with the duniya. we have become so involved in this physical and material world. that we have forgetten who our lord is. And that's why Allah SWT. sent us messengers. to remind us who our master is. so that we can return. so that we can return to him.

See we as Muslims today. as sulemanis. our hearts are dead. We went into auto-pilot a long time ago. And now we're into this state called "Ghafla". or heedlessness. And that's what a pilot does when he gets up in the air. If you ever open that cockpit door. you'll have a heart attack. because they're snoring. they're in deep sleep. and the plane is on auto pilot. And that's what has happened to us. We've gone into this mechanical mode. We say Allah Akbar. Allah is great. without actually meaning it in anyway. We start our namaz and few minutes later forget which rakaat we're in. Did i do one sajda or two sajda?

That is why in Islam. everything begins with an intention.  A niyah? Why is that?. Because the intention controls the action.  And where does intention lie?
محلُ النِّیَّۃ القلب
The place of intention is in the heart. But we say the niyah. and we go into auto pilot. This is what we do. We go to sleep. And then there are different levels and degress  of sleep. That's why Maulana Ali said. الناس النیام. People of this world are in a state of sleep.

Someone asked Maulana Ali..... What is all of this? What is all this creation? ..... He replied.....
ھباءٌ لا یُرَا اِلَّا حِ‏ینَ ما یَقَعُ عَلَیہِ النُّورَ اللہ
This is like the dust in the air that only becomes visible when the light of Allah strikes it..... You see this was an Arif Billah..... He was a man who knews Allah SWT..... What a profound description of the atomic realm ..... All of these atoms that are floating in this etherial space that Alah has created..... And the only reason it is visible is because Allah is Noor AsSamaawat wal Ard..... Allah illuminated the heavesn and the earth ..... And heart is the organ of the human being that is capable of perceiving that light in the most profound and deep ways.....

Our hearts.  is a precious gift from Allah . and we have to protect this precious gift. Just look where he put it. He put it behind the sternum. the strongest thing in the body . and then he put a rib cage around it. he didn't put it like the other organs that are out there. where a person has no protection. And that's why if you want to bring down a man in martial arts. that's the first thing they teach you. Go for the groin. because there is no protection. because both physicaolly and spiritually. it is the most inferior ranking organ of the body. But for the heart. Allah put a protection there. A powerful protection. And then he gave it . this oscilating power . in which the blood is flowing in every single cell of the body with every pulse. That's the reason . blood reaches every single cell of the body.

And if you know anything about oscilatory physics. that's a big word I just learnt so I'm using it. Oscilitory physics is the study of entrainment. how things entrain with other things. The word entrain means. when there is a rhythym. which causes other things to gradually fall into synchronism with it. When the rhythym of oscillating device. synchronizes the rhythym of another device. that's called entrainment .

We know soldiers entrain naturally . when they march. It becomes hard to walk with someone out of step. You naturally entrain and sync up with the steps of the other person.right? And that's why soldiers naturally entrain. And this entrainment results in amplification. which is even more frightning. You know what happens to a bridge when soldiers walk in entrainment. right? . That's the first thing taught to every engineer. When soldiers walk on a bridge in one step. the force of the weight . gets amplified exponentially. because it was all together. you see. And that's the power of being unified. When you are unified. you have power. And when you are disunited. you are weak.

Now the heart. the beats of the spiritual heart. understanding the meaning of this beat of La Ilaha Illa Allah. it entrains the mind, the body and the soul. And when this entrainment occurs. when everything gets synchrnoized. there is exponential amplification in the form of actions. The intensity of every action changes.
And that's why Rasul Allah said.

in the body is something

Know that in the body of a human being. there is a lump of flesh. if it is sound the entire body is sound. and if it is corrupt the entire body is corrupt. why? Because the entire body is in entrainment with the heart. If the heart is diseased. the entrainment will be diseased. If the heart is sound. the entrainment will be sound. La Ilaha Illa Allah. is the constant entrainment for the soul. Look at salaah. which is the practical application of this testimony. La Ilaha Illa Allah. The heart is towards Allah . and the body is towards Qibla. Everything is being entrained all at the same time. And then there is the sajda. the ultimate contraction. I told you. sajdah in ancient arabic means when the camel relinquishes its control. and submits to the will and command of its master. In Sajda we are contracting the entire body. bringing everything close together. isnt' that the case?. And we are placing our foreheads. that which contains our intellect. we place that intellect on the ground . And we do that. to elevate the heart. That's an incredible gesture. During our day to day life. when we are standing up or sitting down. the intellect is always above the heart isn't it? . Sajda in salaah. is a deliberate action.  of lowering the intellect in order to elevate the heart. it is symbol. that highlights the precedence of the heart OVER the intellect. SubhanAllah. There is not a single action in this beautiful relgion that is without profound wisdom in it.

And so we really need to protect our hearts. we have to protect this amazing gift of Allah. because just as the physical heart dies. the spiritual heart can also die. And so in order to keep the spiritual heart alive. we have the knowledge of this Quran. This is why we have
تلاوۃ القرآن بالتدبر
Reading the Quran with reflection. And this is important. becausae you need to know why. Why things happen?. Once you understand why Allah AWJ has asked us to do these things. it strengthens the heart . and you develop a desire to fulfill that thing. Because you are now using your intellect. and when the intellect is behind something. it is not the same . as when you are just following something blindly. To believe with conviction is a powerful belief. When there is conviction. there is action. And when there is action. there is change. And so you will change. and things around you will change. So the recitation of Quran with understanding and reflection what keeps the heart alive .


 




 

 

http://www.evolutionnews.org/2016/07/why_similaritie103028.html

For example, if some future paleontologist were to unearth two species of Volkswagens, he might find it plausible that one evolved gradually from the other. He might find the lack of gradual transitions between automobile families more problematic, for example, in the transition from mechanical to hydraulic brake systems, or from manual to automatic transmissions, or from steam engines to internal combustion engines. But if he thought about what gradual transitions would look like, he would understand why they didn't exist: There is no way to transition gradually from a steam engine to an internal combustion engine, for example, without the development of new, but not yet useful, features. He would be even more puzzled by the huge differences between the bicycle and motor vehicle phyla, or between the boat and airplane phyla. But heaven help us when he uncovers motorcycles and hovercraft, the discovery of these "missing links" would be hailed in all our newspapers as final proof that all forms of transportation arose gradually from a common ancestor, without design.

AQL (Intellect)

The عقل (intellect) is essential for processing information that a person receives from a myriad of sources. This information is then used to develop a person's knowledge, understanding and wisdom. 

Especially important in this process is the opportunity to know about the Divine through indirect knowledge from the universe, and direct knowledge from revelation and Prophets.

Ghazali defines عقل as the "force of knowledge ('ilm) of the real nature of things," as well as "that which perceives knowledge." The ‘aql  is therefore the seat of perception.

Nasr (1979, p. 1) adds that according to Islamic terminology the single term, العقل is used to denote both reason and intellect. He explains that although the two are distinct and inter-related, reason is however dependent upon intellect. He further elaborates that the Arabic term العقل stems from the root ‘ql  which means to bind. He asserts that in Islamic theology it is the عقل (intellect) which binds a person to God. The `aql  is thus critical to maintaining connectivity to the Divine.
 
Depending on whether it is used positively or negatively, the intellect can cause a person to rise above animality or stoop below stupidity. Akhir (2012, p. 718) explains that the 'aql  (intellect)that has been bestowed on humans provide them with unique abilities such as reflection and memory recall. The عقل is inherently pure and dignified, and distinguishes humans from other creatures. He asserts that if used appropriately it can ennoble the person; but if misused indiscordance with Divine tenets, will eventually cause the individual to be disgraced.
 
Nasr (1979, p. 2) asserts that in the religious sciences, a person must exercise his/her"intelligence in order to understand God’s revelation." Revelation then enables the intellect to be illuminated and to function properly especially in the acquisition of the Divine truth. He argues that the amalgamation of the intellect and revelation makes it possible for the mind to engage intuition and faith in order know the truth i.e., “the meaning of religion and more particularly God’s word as contained in the Holy Quran.” (Nasr, 1979, p. 3)
 Both intuition and faith provide avenues for the acquisition knowledge without inference or the use of reason.
 

So we discussed that العقل is a flexible container for علم or knowledge, that expands as knowledge increases...
And as we learnt from Rasul Allah (SAS) Hadees.... that it was the first thing that Allah created.... it was the BEST and the loftiest thing that Allah created...and after its creation, Allah gave it complete freedom of choice....
اول ما خلق اللہُ العقل، فقال لہ اقبل فاقبل، و قال لہ ادبر فادبر
Let's explore this hadees a bit further... linquistically...

The first thing we notice is that the hadees states that it was the very first thing that Allah created... اول ما خلق اللہُ العقل Now why is that important? If you have a lot of tasks that you need to complete for school.... what do you do? You prioritize them, right? What are the things you take into consideration when setting your priorities? Urgency and Importance could be one of them... length of the tasks in comparison to the available time... But the first thing you do, is what? create the task list itself before you start actually working on the tasks.... You have to write 6 different papers for your school.... You have all the reference books stacked up on your table... you have a list of all the links to the sites that you're going to need... You have your priority set in terms of which paper you will write first... You have your supply of cookies and your cadburry eggs next to you.... you've chosen the playlist of the tunes you're going to play as you work on these papers... Awesome... Time to being... Time to write the first paper... what do you need? A pen! If you don't have a pen... does anything else matter?? .... except the cadburry eggs of course? No! It's the very first thing you need in order for you to write you papers....

So Allah has created the heaves and the earths and everything that exists in it... He's created these mansions upon mansions in heaven, and he's created the hell.... he's planned out his messengers... I'm going to send Adam first, then Nooh, then Ibrahim, then Moosa, then Eisa (علیھم السلام). And then finally I'm going to send my beloved prophet Muhammad (SAS) with this extremely elaborate and extremely eloquent and symmetrically miraculous message for my creations so that they can understand it and earn my reward and get closer to me and my love... oh wait... what is the first thing they need in order to do that? The intellect! Without the intellect nothing else is going to matter! without the intellect he's not going to recognize my messenger, nor his message... He will not be able to see the miracles in the message that I've placed for him...and without the message he will not be able to come close to me.... he will not be able to get into this beautiful paradise that I've created for him...

Therefore what was the first thing that Allah created? The intellect! SubhanAllah!

Now if intellect is the first thing Allah created.... what is the first thing we should be using to understand his religion? The intellect! So now you tell me... how could I have agreed to responses like don't use your intellect... don't think too much about religion... it'll drive you nuts.... things like that? How could I have accepted that? Before any decision we make in our lives... how can we not present it before the intellect first? Is this going to benefit me in the long run? Or is it going to hurt me in the long run? Do I need this instant pleasure right before me? Or do I want the lasting eternal pleasure that is to come....




The word اقبل comes from the root word قِبل, which means to face something or someone... From that same root word we get the word Qibla... as in Ka'aba is our qibla.... why? Because we turn our face towards it in prayer....  أقبل is an imperative verb.... it's used when giving a command..... a command to someone to either face them.... or step forward facing towards them...... So If I say for example:
أقبَلَ علیہ بالسَّیف it means I'm commanding someone... Advance towards him with a sword!!!!
ادبر on the other hand, means to walk away from somone.... with their back turned towards them...
