So if I tell you to step forward, you can move towards me, while facing me... and if said move back, you could also move back while still facing me... but that would not be ادبر ... The word ادبر suggests, to actually turn around and walk away from me.... showing me your back... So when Allah commanded the intellect to move towards him by saying أقبل ... it walked towards Him... and when He commanded it to move back by saying ادبر .... it literally turned around and walked away from Allah, showing Allah its back... And what does it mean to turn your back? It means to either refuse something, to stop being involved in something, to reject something. So what the hadees states is that when Allah created this superior entity that He called the intellect... He created it with the potential to accept the truth.... but also to turn away from it.... It had the capability to do both... It could walk towards Allah... it could also turn its back on Allah and walk away from Allah.... You understand?

Other narrations extend this hadees further to say.... that after commanding the intellect to move forward and move backward... Allah also said
ثم قال لہ اَقعُدْ فَقَعَدَ ثم قال لہ قُم فَقَام
After that Allah ordered the intellect to sit down and so it sat down... then Allah ordered it to rise ۔۔ and so it stood up... Meaning what? Become passive or become active....  So this hadees suggests 4 possibilities of the intellect....
First to move towards Allah.... second to turn around and move away from Allah.... third is to sit down, which means to compromise for the greater good like what Imam Hassan (AS) did. He compromised with Mua'wiyah for the protection of Islam.... and the fourth to stand its ground no matter what, as in what Imam Hussain (AS) did in Karbala against Yazeed, also for the protection of Islam....

So after giving the intellect these 4 potentials Allah informed the intellect about its importance... and the role it is going to play in the lives of those who will receive this gift.... Allah said...
و عزتي و جلالي! ما خلقت خلقاً أعظمُ منک ولا اَطوعُ منک
I swear by my honor and superiority! I have not created anything more superior or obedient than you!
The first thing to notice here is that Allah out of His 99 names.... chose two particular names to swear by... عزتي و جلالي By my power and my superiority... Now the question is why did he choose these 2 names in particular... It's because this intellect that he created..... was a representative of these two names... Honor and Superiority!
1. A man is honored and dignified in this world and the hereafter by his intellect.... and the arabic word عزۃ represents honor and dignity.... that's why he swore by عزتي . 
2. And the intellect is also the most superior thing that Allah created as he himself declared: ما خلقت خلقاً اعظم منک  I have not created created anything more suprior than you.... and this supriority is from his جلالۃ. That's why he swore by his superiority... و جلالي
Then he said: ولا اَطوعُ منک I have not created anything more obedient than you.. Now let's understand this word obedience becuase it can have a negative connotation to it. The word obedience, especially in the context of Islam means: willfull compliance with an order, request, or law. It also means willing submission to an authority. Now this is really important to understand.... because obedience can also be achieved unwillingly... Obedience can be forced through fear... You can scare someone into obedience ... and it can also be doctored through hope of a reward... for example, I can say if you obey what I'm asking you to do, then I'll give you something in return as your reward....
InshaAllah we'll cover the topic of obedience in detail later on when we talk about love..... For now I just want you to understand.... that the obedience Allah talks about in this hadees.... is the obedience that stems from pure love.... It is WILLING submission of the soul to the command of Allah.... purely out of deep rooted love for him... because of accepting him as an الہ.... not because of fear or hope...

So Allah informed the intellect that obedience or submission is part of your nature.... because I have not created anything more obedient than you... ولا اَطوعُ منک. The only quesiton remains... is who will you be obedient to? Who will you surrender yourself to? Will you be obedient to My message and My messenger? Or will you be obedient to Iblees and his messengers? That.... I will leave up to you... But know that you will definitely submit....  You will either return to me as my obedient followers ; or you will return to me as Shaitaans slaves... What you choose is up to you...
بک أُعبَدُ و بک أُعصیٰ
You can choose to worship me, or you can chose to disobey me... Then He said
بک أُبدي و أُعيد، و لک الثواب و عليک العقاب
I am beginning my creation with you... making you their guide... giving you the capability to accept truth or reject it..... compromise with your desires... or stand firmly on your belief..... and I'm granting you complete freedom of choice.... But know that based on how you guide the souls.... they will either receive my rewards.... or they will incur my wrath... Bascially YOU will be responsible for their success and you will be responsible for their desctruction....

Does everyone understand everything so far?

Now there are a couple of questions that arise at this point.... I'll state the questions first and then let's discuss them individually...

1. Why did Allah give the intellect complete freedom? Isn't that the root of all problems? We make bad choices because we CAN make bad choices.... Allah could have just restricted our intellects to do good..... couldn't He? He could have programmed it only to follow good and turn away from evil....  So then why didn't he??? Wouldn't that have been perfect for us?
Program is the definition of a machine. It would have created a slave mentality. Allah wants us to come to Him with love not by force
To distinguish the pious from the wretched
Example of microwave

2. He says all reward and punishment will depend on the choices that the intellect makes...  What is this reward He's referring to? Jannah right?  And what is the punishment he's talking about? Hell! So why not just create everyone in Jannah? After all he did create Jannah for us human beings only right? So why didn't he just do that? Why did he associate this reward and punishment with the intellect?
Justice to all creation
Reward is defined as: something given in recognition of one's service, effort, or achievement.
And if Jannah is a reward, then by definition of the word reward... it has to be earned...
Jannah is a place of ranks - ranks demand effort and achievements.... and achievements depend on intellect...
In a school system.. you have grades based on how students perform... but babies in nurseries don't have ranks... they're all equal.... A nursery teacher never says anything like you're baby just topped among all babies... because topped in what? Maybe pooping in diapers? There is no intellect at that level... therefore... no effort, no achievements to be graded on...
And Justice dictacates that reward has to be based on achievement

Why not declare all students in a class as toppers?
Why not pay the doctors just as much as the clerks?
In a company why not have all employees get an equal salary?


So what have we discussed so far about the intellect?
1. It is a container for knowledge that expands as the knowledge increases
2. It was the first thing that Allah created... It was the loftiest.... the most superior creation of Allah..... created for the purpose of obedience (willing submission to Allah). It was created with the potential to obey or disobey with complete freedom of choice... And this is the stance that Islam takes in its message... the stance of choice!

وَقُلِ ٱلْحَقُّ مِن رَّبِّكُمْ ۖ فَمَن شَآءَ فَلْيُؤْمِن وَمَن شَآءَ فَلْيَكْفُرْ
My beloved messnger! You reveal the truth to them about their lord... Then whoever wishes can believe and whoever wishes can disbelieve....
And why take this stance? Because Islam is a religion for the intellect... and the intellect has been given choice... therefore people of the intellect must also be given choice... SubhanAllah!


Then we discussed in the previous session that ..... Aql is also a scale.... It can not only choose between good and evil... it can also choose between two goods and two evils.... Faced with the choice between 2 goods, it can choose the greater good.... and given the choice between 2 evils... it can choose the lesser evil....

Now in life what dictates choice? Knowledge!
We did the exercise in which I compared the intllectual choices between a movie and namaaz as a choice between two goods.... We discussed why choosing to watch a movie was a more intellectually convincing choice over namaaz! I chose watching a movie over namaaz for 2 reasons...
1. Its reward, the pleasure that I will derive from the movie is visible. I have complete knowledge of it through previous expereiences..... and
2. The reward is instant... I can immediately get the reward...

In comparison namaaz lost.... why?
1. because its reward is invisible.... I don't have any tangible experience of that reward .... I have some superficial knowledge of it.... but not a real experience of it.... like I have of watching the movies... because I exactly know what the pleasure of enjoying a movie feels like... but I don't know what the reward of namaaz is going to feel like..
2. The reward is delayed... I have to wait another whatever years before I can die and then get that reward.... that is if such a reward even exists....

So between this comparison obviously... watching the movie wins hands down....

Now for the namaaz to win over the movie.... what are the things that need to change? What would have to change so that given the choice between movie and namaaz... I am willing to choose namaaz over movie....

What is it that will allow me to deny an instant gratification for a delayed one?
The pleasure of namaaz needs to be intellectually visible... it has to be greater than the pleasure of watching the movie....
And the reward.... if it is going to be distant.... it needs to be far far greater than the reward I will gain from watching the movie.
And both.... the pleasure and the reward.... has to be longer lasting than the pleasure of the movie.... Because the only time we are happy to delay our gratification is when? When the reward is far greater than the reward of the instant gratification.... 
But for that there are 2 things involved....
1. a clear knowledge of that reward and
2. the trust that the reward will be delivered to you...

Knowledge and trust! And since WITH knowledge comes trust... therefore knowledge then becomes the key component....
Let's say you have to miss school because of something really important (like new Assassins creed came out) .... but you need the lecture notes for that day... Now can you think of a friend in your class who you can trust to take down those notes for you, if he says he would? And Can you think of a friend who you CANNOT trust to take down notes for you even if he said he would...
Why? What is it that allowed you to make that distinction?

So knowledge about a person... his character... his previous dealings with you or others.... that is what creates trust.. right? Knowledge about the person and his track record! The better the track record, and the more knowledge you have of that track record.... the more you what? Trust that person!

So now if we have to trust Allah what do you need? Knowledge about Allah and knowledge about his track record!

Because does Allah ask us to put our trust in him? OMG ridiculous amounts of trust! Because He wants to make a completely opposite deal with us.. We choose something when?  When the reward is visible and instant, right? But Allah says....



So let's study this ayah piece by piece.... No doubt about it... Allah has purchased from the believers, their souls and their wealth, in exchange for heaven. Who has he purchased the souls of? Not everybody... just believers.... And who are the believers? What are the signs of a believer?  Allah continues to explain



They fight in the cause of Allah? Meaning Jihad? So who are these believers fighting? their own our souls! And in fighting them, what do they do? They kill and are killed.... Kill who? Kill their desires! Kill their greed, their impatience, their anger, their desire for revenge.... their need for instant gratifications... and what else do they do? they get killed? Meaning what? What happens when you are killed? You suffer, right? Is it pleasurable to give up your desires, or is it painful? is it painful to see all your friends partying and having fun and enjoying themselves... and you can't because Allah has forbidden you? Of course it is! Is it painful to hold back your anger even when you have the BEST come back that can make everyone go "OHHH SNAP".... Absolutely! So what is Allah saying?

He's saying ... no doubt Allah has purchased from the believers, their souls and their wealth, in exchange for heaven... And the believers I'm talking about are the ones who fight in the cause of Allah, and they kill or are killed.... Meaning, in following the path of Islam and the teachings of their prophet.... they fight their overwhelming desires, their need for instant gratifications and in the process willingly choose to suffer if they have to....
وَعْدًا عَلَيْهِ حَقًّۭا فِى ٱلتَّوْرَىٰةِ وَٱلْإِنجِيلِ وَٱلْقُرْآنِ ۚ وَمَنْ أَوْفَىٰ بِعَهْدِهِۦ مِنَ ٱللَّهِ
This is a promise that Allah has made compulsory on Himself... The promise of this reward of heaven is something that Allah has made compulsory on Himself.... not just in the Quran, but in the books before it.. the old testamenets and the bible....
وَمَنْ أَوْفَىٰ بِعَهْدِهِۦ مِنَ ٱللَّهِ
And who is more true in his promise than Allah?
This ayah is so beautiful... notice when Allah talked about the promise... he talked about Himself.... not the believers... Meaning he did not use the word PROMSIE when he talked about the believers... he used the word purchase.... but he used the word promise for Himself! Why? Because believers can make mistakes... they can falter.... but Allah does not make mistakes.... He cannot make mistakes... but the believers can.... So even if the believers make mistakes... they falter on their purchase.... Allah says... as long as they keep trying... my purchase, my deal will remain valid.... SubhanAllah.... He imposed perfection on Himself... not the believers...



So this is the first part of the Ayah... Now let's explore this part in a bit more detail.... This ayah begins with a transaction...  a business deal if you will..  إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ ٱشْتَرَىٰ In which Allah has purchased! From whom? مِنَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ from believers... What did he purchase? أَنفُسَهُمْ their souls and وَأَمْوَلَهُم  their wealth, and in return He is giving us what?  What are we receiving for this transaction? بِأَنَّ لَهُمُ ٱلْجَنَّةَ We are getting heaven.  This is the exchange Allah talks about in this ayah..

Now what is the ayah teaching us? In order to understand this... let's just talk about the idea of a sale for a moment... Because He is after all talking about a transaction is He not? And a transaction is one in which you sell something and someone purchases what you're selling... Allah says I'm purchasing your soul and your wealth.... The 2 things most dear to all human beings...

Now first of all, can you purchase something from me if I'm not willing to sell it to you? No! So the fact that Allah is purchasing means what? That I'm willing to sell it... You understand? It's a mutual transaction in which both parties are in agreement... It's not a forced transaction...

Now we learnt .... that we human beings... just by nature... we love immediate transactions... what the Quran calls تجارتاً حاضرۃ . right? In other words I pay for something now and I get it when? Right now! The only time we like to delay a transaction, is when we say I want the product now... I want the big screen TV now, but I will pay over a period of 6 months... That's the only time we are fine with delaying our payments.... We like to pay over time..... but receive Immediately... otherwise usually when we pay now, we expect the product right away!

So human beings like to pay late but receive immediately... that's our nature. you agree?

In this ayah, Allah says.. you give me your soul, and you give me your wealth NOW, and in return I will give you heaven...
Now do we like to receive immediately or later? We like to receive immediately. Is Jannah coming immediately or later? It's coming later.... but when do you have to make the payments to Allah? Now! And do you have to make this payment one time or do you have to keep making it everyday? Everyday! We have to keep making those payments with our time, with our wealth, with our prayers, with our fasts, with the way we live, the way we dress, the way we talk, by giving up the things we want to do, but can't do because they're haraam, with the money we can't earn because it's not the permissible way of earning it ... the money we want to spend but it's not the right way to spend that money... and we are constantly holding ourself back because we are in a transaction with Allah...

And we have to constantly remind ourselves that we are doing this in exchange for what? Jannah! But human nature is that if you're paying a price.... if you're making sacrifice to earn something you wanna see it right away! Even if you work 40 hours at a job... atleast 2 weeks later you see a paycheck .... you're going to see something... but when Allah purchases our souls and wealth from us in exchange for Jannah... do we see jannah? NO! All we get is a verbal confirmation from Allah... I'll give it to you... trust me! that's all we get..... nothing else... Allah says.. OK i'll describe it to you in my book... it's got trees, it's got lots of fruits... it's got rivers , you can get married and stuff...  we get a lot of description of jannah.. but do we have a catalog? a broucher, a picture, maybe a video ? A short trailer... just give me a short trailer of jannah ... I don't want the whole movie, just show me like a 30 seconds clip of it.... so that i know what's coming.... nothing! All you get is Allah's word!

Now let's stop here for a second and take a step back and think about what Allah is saying here... He is this salesman, who's coming to us and saying...  Listen, I have this amazing property... prime real estate location... it's like 50 acres, it's a mansion! ... It's worth 50 million, but I'm going to sell it to you for 5000 bucks.... All you have to do is pay me the 5000 now, that too in small tiny installment, and in about 50 or 70 years I'll tell you where that property is... But this deal is only open right now... If you agree... the mansion is yours!

I'll give it to you when? After 50 years... but you have to pay me Right now! Now if any real estate dealer came to us and gave us this deal.... what would be our reaction? We'd be like you sleezy scam artist.... Get outa here... don't waste my time....

But this is Allah... our creator... our master making this deal with us.... Allah is saying I'll give you Jannah, when you're being laid into the ground and there is dirt being thrown on your face.... when you're in the ground... that's when I'll give it to you.... but when do you pay Him? Right now... and we're like ... ummmmmm and he says "Trust Me"... Just trust me.... Doesn't this take an incredible amount of trust?  That's what we mean when we say "believing in the unseen".

ٱلَّذِينَ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱلْغَيْبِ وَيُقِيمُونَ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ وَمِمَّا رَزَقْنَهُمْ يُنفِقُونَ۔ وَٱلَّذِينَ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِمَآ أُنزِلَ إِلَيْكَ وَمَآ أُنزِلَ مِن قَبْلِكَ وَبِٱلْآخِرَةِ هُمْ يُوقِنُونَ
That's what we mean when we say we believe and trust Allah... It is not an easy thing.... It is a very easy thing to say it.... that I trust Allah.... but it is a very difficult thing to do... And that's why Allah immediately reassures us...
وَعْدًا عَلَيْهِ حَقًّۭا فِى ٱلتَّوْرَىٰةِ وَٱلْإِنجِيلِ وَٱلْقُرْآنِ ۚ وَمَنْ أَوْفَىٰ بِعَهْدِهِۦ مِنَ ٱللَّهِ
Trust me my slave! Check out my track record from previous books.... I have a great track record... 100% perfection... I have never failed on my promise.... never did... never will.... Trust me!

And why do we need this assurance? Because when you walk this path.... EVERYONE around you will say you are a fool! You're missing out on life! You could have been doing so much more with your youth....  You're wasting your life away with your Islamic values... and that scruffy beard you're trying to grow... You have to go to the masjid and pray and this and that..... we could be having so much fun during that time ...  You only live once! You know...
You're starting your college life... You'll be living in the dorm, your parents won't even be around ... you'll be all by yourself.. There's going to be so much PARTY man! Everyone one is going to have the time of their lives...... I think your lab partner likes you too! It's all good.... Don't worry about it... You're a good lucking guy... you're a beautiful woman... you can have anything and everything you ever wanted.... you have such a beautiful body... you need to show it! You've got  6 pack abbs man.... you need to be more like Salman Khan... take off your shirt for no reason at all.. It's OK... It's not even Ramadan yet..

Now Shaitaan on the other hand gives us the exact opposite deal... He says listen listen! I got some amazing duniya, some luxuries , some incredible pleasures for you... sure they're inferior.... but you can get them RIGHT NOW.... just do this... just drink this... just smoke this....  just steal it, just click on this link.... it's right there.. so easy! Sure You might get into trouble later... but it ain't gonna be right now... you're good! you're fine.! At least for the next 30 50 years there's not going to be any problems ....  And he comes to us and offers us immediately visible reward, with no payments... Obviously far more tempting... far more powerful.. to take immediate reward and leave off what is going to come so far later...

And because we are impulsive buyers... and when a juicy product comes in front of us... we just HAVE to put it in the cart... we can't hold ourselves.... So shaitan makes us the sale and most of us do what? We become customers... we become immediate customers...  But Allah makes us a promise.... and because we don't SEE the product .... so what do we say? eeaaaahhh maybe Ramadan.... that is if the new x-men movie doesn't come out....  I know it's ramadan... but I really wanna go watch the movie... and I can't watch movie without snacks... I'm in such a religious dilemma ... I don't know how to reconcile these two things?

And you say NO... I've made a sale with Allah... I've made a transaction with Allah... And they'll go.... you're SO stupid man! What world are you living in? This is 2015 bro! Why can't you get smart....


And then you keep listening to this.... seeing all this stuff around you and eventually there comes a point where you start feeling ... I can't take this pressure anymore. Everyone is telling me I'm being stupid and missing out on life.... Am I really making a mistake? Am I really missing out on all the fun? Am I really a fool?  Then you open up the Quran and read this ayah and Allah tells you....
فَٱسْتَبْشِرُوا۟ بِبَيْعِكُمُ ٱلَّذِى بَايَعْتُم بِهِ وَذَلِكَ هُوَ ٱلْفَوْزُ ٱلْعَظِيمُ
Congratulation my believer! You made a great sale! You are the one who is going to experience the ultimate, the greatest of all success....
The only one to congratulate you in these tryin gtimes is not going to be your friends... The only one to congratulate you will be Allah! Your congratulations will come from no where else ... nobody else will think you're making a wise decision.

When you trust Allah... let me tell you... Sometimes the world will stand against you... And you will get so disheartened, so frustrated... you will start losing hope.... At that time you need to grab hold of Allah's book and read it.... and when you read it you will hear Allah saying... Congratulations my slave! You've made an excellent trade...  This is me promising you your reward....

May Allah grant me and you, and everyone of us the wisdom to take His promise seriously... Maybe you've made many many mistakes in the past..... But today you can say... I want to make this sale now... What has happened has happened.... I will not let my past determine my future..... I am willing to choose this path now from today onwards.... And if i fall short... if I make mistakes... it's OK, I will again get back on this deal....

People will give up on you all the time... Best Friends will give up on you.... even your own parents may give up on you... your spouses may give up on you.... but Allah never gives up on you... Allah will never ever give up on you... People will judge you and condemn you.... you know what... you've broken your word too many times... that's it the deal is off!  End of story... but Allah will NEVER NEVER close His doors on you... The only one who can close those doors is you! YOU can close these doors on yourself.... Allah will never do it! SubhanAllah!

And this is what I want to show you kids in these session.... what Allah wants for us.... How much he loves us... How much he cares for us.... How He is the ONLY true friend you and I will have.... But for that we need to gain this knowledge from the Quran... so that the reward of afterlife becomes visible to us... and we understand how the reward is so much greater than the fleeting instant pleasures of this world....

Because this world is DUNIYA.... which I told you comes from the word "dana" meaning low (as in easily accessible, instant) but inferior... Whereas AKHIRAH comes from the word Khair which means superior... So Hayaat AlDuniya means The Inferior life even though it is instant..... whereas Haayat AlAkhirah means The Superior life, even though it comes later.... But everything worth anything always demands patience....

Yes... the rewards of this deal come later... but if you honestly notice... the rewards of everything worth anything are always received later....

School life compared to professional life is inferior or not? Why? Because you're dependant.... and professional life is superior why? Because you're independant! But to achieve this independant superior life... where must you strive hard? Where must you make sacrifices of pleasures? In your school life....
Being an adult is better or being a child? An adult! But you have to invest in those 18 years of training to reap its rewards laster....
Is it better to have one mango.... or is better or having a tree of mangoes ? Having a tree of course! loaded with mangoes.... but do you get that instantly as soon as you plant the seeds or do you have to wait for it? You have to wait for it? But is the reward manyfold greater than that instant gratification of one mango? Absolutely!
Is it better to cut open the goose to get all the golden eggs today? Or feed that goose and keep it alive and delay that gratification by getting one golden egg everyday?
Is it better to enjoy all the money you have today... or is it wiser to invest it in stocks... or long term saving accounts in order to collect double or tripple the amount of the original investment?
So the question before me and before you is..... Are these few temporary years of this duniya... of this inferior life better? Or is the everlasting superior life of Akhira better? Akhira! But where do you have to strive in order to reap the rewards in that superio life? In this inferior life

وَمَآ أُوتِيتُم مِّن شَىْءٍۢ فَمَتَعُ ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَزِينَتُهَا ۚ وَمَا عِندَ ٱللَّهِ خَيْرٌۭ وَأَبْقَىٰٓ ۚ أَفَلَا تَعْقِلُونَ
And whatever thing you have been given - it is only for the enjoyment of this inferior life and its adornment. And what is with Allah is so much better and ever lasting;
And then Allah asks us...
  أَفَلَا تَعْقِلُونَ so why don't you understand?  What tool do you need to understand or reason? The intellect! SubhanAllah!

بَلْ تُؤْثِرُونَ ٱلْحَيَوٰةَ ٱلدُّنْيَا؟ وَٱلْآخِرَةُ خَيْرٌۭ وَأَبْقَىٰٓ
Do You really prefer the life of this inferior world? Even though the after life is so much better and ever lasting!
إِنَّ هَذَا لَفِى ٱلصُّحُفِ ٱلْأُولَىٰ۔ صُحُفِ إِبْرَهِيمَ وَمُوسَىٰ
I've said these same things in the previous books....in the books of Ibrahim and Moosa... This is not something new I'm telling to you about..... I've always been telling you this.... from the beginning of time that the afterlife is so much better than the life of this world.... why don't you guys understand? When will you understand?
So intellect is the first thing Allah created....
We also learnt that it is the most lofty thing that Allah created for the purpose of obedience. It had the potential to obey or disobey Allah... It had the potential to compromise and it had the potential to stand its ground... And Allah gave it complete freedom of choice...
Intellect is a scale..... it can not only choose between good and bad, it can also choose between two goods and two evils. In the last session I talked in detail about this....

The way in which the intellect works is that when faced with 2 good choices.... it chooses the pleasure or the benefit that is visible and instantly accessible... The only time it chooses something not visible and something accessible later is when...
1. When there is knowledge of the reward that is to come in the future... and absolute trust in the one who has promised the reward
2. When the benefit and the reward that is to come... far exceeds the benefit of the instant gratification

And based on this trust we accepted the deal that Allah made with us....
إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ ٱشْتَرَىٰ مِنَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ أَنفُسَهُمْ وَأَمْوَلَهُم بِأَنَّ لَهُمُ ٱلْجَنَّةَ
We sold our souls and our wealth to Allah...... as believers... in exchange for his promise of heaven....
يُقَتِلُونَ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ فَيَقْتُلُونَ وَيُقْتَلُونَ
We agreed to the terms and conditions of being a believer as Allah imposed on us.... my believers are those who will sacrifice their worldly desires, their visible and instantly gratifying desires for me.... and they will be willing to suffer for it.... It's not going to be easy for them.... There will be times when killing their desires will mean literally cutting their heart out.... but they will do it... for me
وَعْدًا عَلَيْهِ حَقًّۭا فِى ٱلتَّوْرَىٰةِ وَٱلْإِنجِيلِ وَٱلْقُرْآنِ ۚ وَمَنْ أَوْفَىٰ بِعَهْدِهِۦ مِنَ ٱللَّهِ
And they can trust me... because I have made this promise of reward mandatory for myself in not only the Quran but all the previous books... to all previous nations.... I'm not asking my believers to promise me that they will never break this deal.... In fact, I am promising them that I will give them this reward no matter how many times they break this deal with me..... just as long as they sincerely and honestly keep trying.... As long as they constantly return to this deal with me.... As long as they don't take undue advantage of my love and mercy.... I am promising you... I will give you this reward...
And when this world forces you to break this deal with me; when your friends force you to break this deal with me; when your family forces you to break this deal with me.... when your desires force you to break this deal with me.... when everyone one around you is calling you a loser... a fool... a fanatic... a coward... and you can't take it anymore.... you can't bear the pressure, you can't bear the pain of those insults.... those criticisms and sarcasms any more.... in that moment of weakness remember my words
فَٱسْتَبْشِرُوا۟ بِبَيْعِكُمُ ٱلَّذِى بَايَعْتُم بِهِۦ وَذَلِكَ هُوَ ٱلْفَوْزُ ٱلْعَظِيمُ
In that moment.... rejoice in the faith... feel congratulated by Me.... your Master and your lord... who loves you more than anyone in this world... who loves you more than your mother, your father, your family, your kids, your spouse, your friends.... all of them COMBINED.... feel congratulated by Him on this amazing deal that you've made.... because you will be the successful one! You will achieve the great fortune....  ٱلْفَوْزُ ٱلْعَظِيمُ.

Did we discuss these words  ٱلْفَوْزُ ٱلْعَظِيمُ in the last session?

The word فوز in Arabic comes from the word فاز. which means success, or a gain, or some good fortune.... But in ancient or classical Arabic, the word فوز is used, when you succeed, or are victorious.... or you gain something.... meeting two specific conditions ..... If either one of these conditions is not met... then it is not called فوز ...
The first condition is that whatever you've achieved... you then never want to part with.... EVER! Not even for a second.... that's the first condition... but that's not all... what you've achieved... as part of فوز..... it should also make you the object of desire for others.... it has to make you the object of envy for others..... When others look at you, they should desperately WANT what you have.... they should go... OMG... I'd give absolutely ANYTHING in a heartbeat to get what s/he has.... that's when the word فوز  is used.....
So there are two nuances to the word فوز .... You have to love it so much that you never want to part with it... and others should crave what you have... only when both these conditions are met... that's when you have فوز

And in this deal... Allah has promised the believer فوز .... He said... if you come through on this deal with Me... I promise... that what I will give you... it will not only make you so happy that you will NEVER EVER want to part from it... it'll also be SO AWESOME that everyone around you today... your friends.. your family... your loved ones.. your enemies... EVERYONE will wish they had what you've been given...

Another beautiful derivation of the word فوز ... is فازَۃٌ, which to the Arabs meant .... an extremely comfortable tent in the middle of a scorching desert.... Now imagine you're an Arab wandering in the scorching heat of the desert..... very thirsty, hungry and tired... ... And you find this nice and cool tent... containinng all the comforts you need.... water, food, comfortable bed... big screen tv... What would that tent, that comfort mean to you at that time? Once you enter it, would it not also satify the previous 2 conditions of the word فوز? Because it would be something you would never want to part with... you'd never want to go back in the scorching heat of the desert.... and all others still in the scorching heat will be craving for what you have gained!

But then in this ayah.... Allah did not just promise us فوز  did he? He promised ... فَوْزُ عَظِيمُ. .... Allah tacked the word عظیم with it.... If I start going into the linguistic nuances of this words... we'll never see the end of this discussion on intellect.... So I'll tell you one small meaning quickly... The word عظیم comes from the word عَظُم, which is also the root word for عظام  which means "bone".... Now what are the attributes of a bone?? It is solid right? Its solid, it's stable, it's strudy... it's reliable... the entire human structure stands upon it... but then there is the word عظامۃ, as a verb, which literally means: when the bone grows strong.... like the bones of a growing child.... you know how a child's bones become stronger as he grows.. So combine it with فوز, the suggestion is a success whose pleasure increases.... and becomes stronger with the passage of time....
Now from a grammatical stand point, the words فَوْزُ عَظِيمُ  in this ayah are used as a noun infinitve phrase.... In grammar an infinitive phrase is something that extends infinitely and indefinitely... something that is subject to no limitation... For example the phrase...

1. Everyone wants to enjoy life.... is a noun infinitive phrase... It's continuous and it is infinitive... The sentence suggest that there will not come a time when everyone will not want to enjoy life... So is the phrase....
2. She likes to be admired. - the desire for admiration is an infinite thing indefinite thing...

So the grammatical use of  فَوْزُ عَظِيمُ  in this ayah suggests that this reward that you achieved, it is not only so amazing that you don't want to ever part with it.. it's not only something that makes you the envy of others... it is not only growing strong as time passes.... but also that its growth is infinite... There's not going to come a time when this reward stops increasing... EVER!!! So how much will the pleasure of this reward increase? It will keep growing.... and growing and growing... !!! Infinitely!!! SubhanAllah.... 

Now you tell me... Isn't this فوز عظیم a great reward?

We said ... the only time we are willing to sacrifice instant gratifications is when? When the reward we are going to get later is much much much greater than the pleasure we are sacrificing right now, correct? You tell me honestly... no matter what we sacrifice in this lifetime... isn't this reward from Allah..... this فوز عظیم that you love so much... and you can't part with... that makes you the  envy of everyone around you because of how magnificent it is... one that grows infinitely ... beyond measure....  and its pleasure never stops increasing... isn't this a greater reward than any sacrifice we will ever make in this world? It certainly is....

But then Allah did not just sayٌفوزٌ عظیم did he? He said الفوز العظیم. He added the ال to both words.... To both فوز and عظیم. AllahuAkbar... ال in Arabic is called علامت المعرفۃ. When you want to make something specific.. like not just any book but a particular book... Not just any car but a particular car.... that's one reason when you add ال  to a word in Arabic... to make it specific....
Allah says for this deal that you're making.... I'm not going to give you فوز, I will give you الفوز.... That ever increasing reward that I just descibed.. it will be specific to you.... Nobody else in my entire creation... will have the experience YOU are going to have... Each one of you will have your own individual experience....
And that experience is not just going to be عظیم, it's going to be العظیم. It's not going to keep increasing infinitely in any generic way... It's going to increase in a way specifically tailored for you.... It'll infinitely increase in the specific way you want it to increase in.... 

This is what I love about the Arabic language.... can you see the depth of this language in just 2 words!

We should all... each and everyone of us.... memorize this ayah with full comprehension... because there are so many times when we need to remind ourselves of this....


What else does the word عقل mean?
It is a restraint! Another meaning for the word عقل in ancient arabic, is to tie or restrain a camel with a strong rope.... And the rope used to tie the camel was called عقال  . Now let's explore the meaning of عقل as a restraint....
Now a camel to an Arab, is what a car is to a teenager.... Your car, no matter what it looks like... what year, make or model it is.... it's precious to you, correct?... That's why you spit shine it twice a day.... right? Now if you love your car so much, would you be worried about its safety?  Of course.... you would be! Why? Because it's YOUR car! It's your baby.... You love it... and it is also your responsibility.... ONLY you can take care of it in the best possible way! You'll make sure it stays in good condition... you get the oil changes done regularly... you take care of its service and maintenance needs.... And all this stems from your sense of ownership and also your attachment to it....

This is what a camel was to the Arab.... It was not only his ride, it was not only its source of livelihood.... it was also his closest friend in most cases.... because these were bedouins who scaled the deserts... They didn't spend as much time with their wives and children as much as they did with their camels... Believe it or not.... they actually wrote long poems about their camels... about its eyes, the way it moved... which should now be of no surprise to you.... because after all poetry was their thing.... Poetry to them.... was what conversation is to us......

So now imagine you've time travelled 2000 years back in time, and your parents just bought you your very own camel... This camel is now your new BFF! So you ride into town on your new camel to buy some groceries.... When you reach the market.... you park your camel outside.... you dismount it...  Now what do you have to do before you enter the market? No you don't pull the hand break!!
You have to tie the camel! Why? Why do you need to tie the camel? Why can't you just leave it there?  Because your camel doesn't know what's good for it and what's not... If you don't tie it... it may wander off..... someone else will find it..... and a few hours later... your best friend might end up on a huge platter.... all nice and tender... possible? But not even just that... you can't actually take care of a camel in anyway until you tie it... You can't feed it...... You can't bathe and clean it...... You can't treat it when it's sick or hurt...... You can't shelter it from the storm... You can't do any of this until you have tied it up first .... Why? Because first you HAVE to restrain it... get it under your control... and then do whatever you need to do to take care of it... You understand? Same thing with your car!

Therefore I argue that restraint is compulsory... its required for safety.... and the benefit of the one being restrained.... Intellect is a restraint... Until you set a boundary... you cannot tackle anything in life... That's why we have to-do lists.... that's why we have specific subjects in school... taught in a particular time frame... We restrain subjects within time frames.....  That's why we have 9-5 work boundaries... That's why we build houses in which we confine ourselves for our safety and our benefit... Everything we do in life... we can do in life is only possible because of some form or restraint or boudary....
Even the universe works on the principle of restraint... Thats why we have seasons bound in a particular time durations... That's why we have a duration for day and a duration for night...  Imagine if day and night happened randomly... our entire life would be destroyed... we would not be able to take advantage of the day, nor would we be able to take advantage of the night...
There is nothing in this world, that can function without a restraint.... In order to give benefit or take benefit... some sort of restraint is required... And anything that goes out of restraint, goes out of control...... and anything that goes out of control is disasterous... What happens when someone loses restraint or control over your car? Accidents! What happens when you lose control over your balance? You fall! What happens when the gas coming to your stove is not restrained or controlled? What happens when the flame of the fire is not restrained, not controlled when you cook your food???
And what happens when nature releases its restraint over its elements like wind and water? You get hurricanes and sunamis...
Therefore I argue that restraint is an essential component to physical human existence...

Does everyone agree with me on this??? Any questions about this?


So returning to the camel... the reason you tie your camel is for its safety.... You want to keep it controlled so that you can keep it safe because you care about it, because its YOUR camel... it's your responsibility... So there are two reasons for tying your camel: love and justice.... You keep it restrained because you love it and you want to care of it... and you keep it restrained because you don't want the camel to hurt others... Not let it harm other peoples crops and property! And this applies to everything in life... Love for self... justice for others... You agree?

However... by tying the camel, did you limit it's freedom? Did you restrict its movement? You did!.... But was it absolutely necessary for you to do that? Yes of course!  Now do you think the camel always likes that? As soon as you reached the market.... does the camel jump up and down and say... Yay! tie me up! Tie me up!! :)) Probably not... But nevertheless you tie it up because you understand that the restraint is necessary for its safety and the safety of others... whether the camel understands it or not... whether it appreciates it or not.... whether it likes it or not... You are doing what you're doing to keep it and others safe..

But yes! Tying the camel does limits the camels freedom... See the thing with safety is..... it is directly proportional to the level of restraint.... or it is inversely proportional to freedom....
Meaning the higher the level of safety required.... the stronger the restraint will be.... To tie the camel you're not going to just use a piece of string, will you? No! You'll use a strong enough rope, right? The restraint will be in proportion to strength of the camel...
And as the level of safety increases... the level of freedom will be limited... The stronger you tie the camel... the more restricted and limited its movement will be...

You sit in your car... what is the first thing you do? You put your seat belt on.... Now as soon as you put it on.... does it restrict your movement to a certain extent? Yes it does.... It doesn't completely incapacitate you, but it does limit your movement! You can still move... just not as freely... But does that restraint save you in the case of an accident? It does InshaAllah!
What about when you sit on a roller coaster ride? Does it completely restrict your movement? It does! Why? because a much much higher level of safety is required!


What about traffic lights? Do they limit freedom? Of course! You have to wait till the light turns green! But are these lights essential to the safety of drivers? Yes!
What happens at airports? You have to go through all these security checks .... does it limit your freedom a LOT? Absolutley.... But those controls are restrictions are required for your and my safety .... as well as the safety of other passengers.... And the greater the level of safety required, the higher the level of what? restrictions!


So now is Islam a restrictive religion? You bet it is! It is definitely a restrictive religion... but why? Because of safety! Whose safety? Your and my safety... Your safety individually and our safety  as a group collectively... We are Allah's creation... We are his camels... Rasul Allah is our shepherd and Islam is the rope of Allah that Rasul Allah (SAS) has used to tie us... so that he can keep us safe.... individually as well as collectively...
Have you ever seen how a rope is made? It is made from two strings twined firmly together.... Allah's rope is made of Islam and Iman twined together.... And he says in the Quran  وَٱعْتَصِمُوا۟ بِحَبْلِ ٱللَّهِ جَمِيعًۭا وَلَا تَفَرَّقُوا۟ Hold fast to the rope of Allah and do not get divided.... what is that rope of Allah? It is the rope of of Islam and Iman...

Islam is a restrictive religion because it is a religion of safety... And there cannot be safety unless there is restraint....  The Arabic word Islam comes from the root word سلم. which has many different meanings... InshaAllah we'll explore all of them at a later time.... One of those meanings is to surrender willingly  and completely... willingly and completely!
إِذْ قَالَ لَهُۥ رَبُّهُۥٓ أَسْلِمْ .  قَالَ أَسْلَمْتُ لِرَبِّ ٱلْعَلَمِينَ
His master said to Ibrahim AS, surrender! He said I completely and willingly surrender to the master of the worlds
And the one who willingly and completely surrenders himself.... his will.... his desires.... his ego to Allah is called a Muslim
أَلَّا تَعْلُوا۟ عَلَىَّ وَأْتُونِى مُسْلِمِينَ
Don't be arrogant before me... Come to me in submission as a Muslim
Another meaning of the word سلام is safety...
قُلْنَا يَا نَارُ كُونِي بَرْدًا وَسَلَامًا عَلَىٰ إِبْرَاهِيمَ
We ordered the fire to become cold and safe for Ibrahim....

So 2 meanings out of many other meanings for the word سلام  are what? Submission and Safety! And how do you become safe? By completely surrending yourself to your master... Can you tie your camel if it keeps struggling? No! The camel needs to surrender to your rope... to your عقال  in order for you to keep it safe.... We are Allah's camels... And Rasul Allah (SAS), is our shepherd..... Islam is his rope that he ties us with.... We need to surrender to the rope he has given us... We need to accept him as our shepherd and surrender to the rope of Islam if we desire OUR safety and if we desire OUR success... Why? Because there can never be safety without restraint.... and we need to surrender willingly and completely to that restraint if we want to be safe.. Safety from what?  From satanic temptations.... Because what happens when a camel starts wandering? It will wander into every single valley that draws its attention....ٰIt'll get lost... It'll get killed...

The rope of Islam forms a secure boundary all around us for our safety, so that Allah.... who is our master can take care of us... He can give us the benefits and the rewards and the success he desires for us....  If we do not surrender ourselves to this boundary of Islam... If we do not secure ourselves within this boundary of Islam.... completely and willingly we are going to lose our way to Allah.... We will wander away from him....
The reason we feel Islam's restrictions so much on us today is because of the times we live in.... Look at the temptations that surround us.... the external pressures we all are exposed to! Outside the restraint of Islam you have magazines and media and people that tell you that you have to dress a certain way... look a certain way... talk a certain way... behave a certain way... If you don't do that you're not cool... you're old fashioned... you're stupid.... We constantly keep feeling this pull while we are tied to the rope of Islam... Of course it's difficult....

But the question before the intellect is... what is the right choice? Instant gratification? Or الفوز العظیم?? Because we are surrounded by satanic temptations all around us.... Everything that is COOL today..... we find it's against the teachings of Islam!! In every dimension of our lives.... The only thing protecting us is the rope of Allah.... the rope of Islam... The only way to save ourselves is to surrender willingly and completely to this عقال ... this restraint of Allah which is Islam....

If a hurricane is coming... what do you do? You surrender yourself to a house... a shelter... that you know has a solid foundation... You admit that I am not strong enough to face this storm by myself.... I need to take shelter in a structure that is stronger than me.....  so that IT can keep me safe... You submit yourself and your freedom in exchange for the protection and safety of that shelter... Yes? What'll happen if you try to stand and face the storm and say... Aahhh I can take it on! I ain't scared of it! How long will you last? But once you're in the shelter are you restricted? Do you have to give up some of your freedom for your safety? Of course!

And what'll happen if once inside the shelter..... you want to keep the doors and windows open?? Yeah I know there is a storm outside... but it's so suffocating in here... let me just open the window.. let me just keep the door open for a nice breeze... It's OK... it's not like I'm standing outside in the open... I'm just one step outside the door! I just have my neck ouside the door.... it's not the end of the world... Don't be so paranoid!

I know Islam forbids it, but so what if I eat a little bit of crab, a little bit of lobster, mushrooms... so what? So what if I smoke... at least its not alcohol.... what's the big deal.... I know what Islam says about clothing, but so what if I dress provocatively... what's the big deal..... it 2015! What's the problem?
Well no problem to Islam really!..... The shelter will still be there AFTER the storm has passed.... The shelter is not the one going to get hurt.... It's you that will get hurt!  Becuase you violated the rules of safety!


Islam is a religion of restraint... a restraint put on us by our master for our souls and intellect..... It is a restraint that Allah placed on us so that we remain connected to Him... because he loves us.... because he cares about us.... He does not want us to wander away from him... He does not want us to leave Him....  Just as we restrict a camel because we care about it and we don't want it to wander away and get lost....  Allah says...

أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّهُمْ فِى كُلِّ وَادٍۢ يَهِيمُونَ
Do you not see that they wander aimlessly in every valley? كُلِّ وَادٍۢ يَهِيمُونَ
يَهِيمُونَ comes from the word ھاما. ھاما is used for a camel when it gets lost in the desert looking for water... It goes in one valley, then another valley, then another valley  searching for water.. aimlessly waandering looking for water... and finally dies of thirst... When the Arabs used the word ھاما it meant that it is pointless waiting for the camel to return, because it is never going to return... This is how Allah describes those of us wandering trying to find a connection elsewhere....

We should really examine our own lives and the lives of those around us in the context of this ayah... We accomplish so much in this world, but why is it that the desires of our heart never end? Some spend their entire time on facebook, whatsapp, all these social sites... desperately trying to find a connection... but the heart keeps longing for more .... constantly keeps searching for more! And no matter how hard we try... why just can't seem to find that perfect connection... We continuously have that feeling of emptiness... that void in our hearts no matter how hard we try to fill it! Some try to fill that emptiness with money, some with power, some try to drown it in alcolhol, some try to forget it with drugs.... but that emptiness is always there... If money is the key to all happiness then why are so many rich people committing suicides? I can understand if the poor runs after wealth, but why do we see the filthy rich people constantly running after money as well? The guy is a millionaire, but his heart still wants more? He wants to be billionarie... Why is that? What is he searching for? Even with all the money, all the comforts of this world... why is his heart never satisfied? Why is it always looking for more? Because they're wandering aimlessly in the valley of greed and deception....
فِى كُلِّ وَادٍۢ يَهِيمُونَ

There was a sage who had the knowledge of turning straw into gold... A man begged him to teach him that skill.... The sage asked him... what will you do with all the gold you make? He said I'll become very rich.... The sage asked why do you want to be rich? He said so I can live a very comfortable and peaceful life.... So the sage said, then it is not gold that you seek... what you really seek is comfort and peace for you heart....  Wouldn't it be better if I taught you how to find peace and comfort for your heart instead of teaching you how to make gold?

Today we have people who think that we gold is the key to finding peace and comfort... it is not.... You can make as much gold as you want.... your heart will never find comfort from it... Yes you can distract it for a while with luxurous items, jewllery, branded cars, big mansions.... but this hunger...this thirst of the heart will never go away.... because the heart is not searching for gold... it's searching for something else....

Look at the artists in the music industry. How they wander and lose themselves in the valley of desire and fame.... The artists starts off with a desire for their song to be heard... She gets a break, someone produces his album... everybody downloads it... it goes platinum... they become extremely popular, extremely wealthy in one night as a result... She wins a music award! Now is that the end of his career? No! Now she has to come up with the next big hit.... but the next album she releases is not as entertaining.... not as successful... not as many sales... didn't make it to the top of the charts... Now she starts feeling desperate.... she starts seeing her own decline.... what does every artist want? Even  more than money? Fame! Appreciation, Acknowledgement, recognition! They live off of that... but instead of that now she's getting criticisms... rotten tomatos, one star, thumbs down.... she starts getting depressed... so she starts thinking what can i do to get back on top? I need to come up with a new song... wait! I already tried that... didn't work out too well... Maybe if my new song was a little controvertial... maybe if it was a little more indecent.. it might get more popular... does that happen? Yes it does!
And then when that doesn't work as well then maybe if I drop my standards of decency even further... maybe that'll get me there... If that doesn't happen... maybe I'll just shave my head... get drunk.. get arrested... adopt a baby... adopt 12 babies....Maybe if I can't be mentioned at the award ceremony.. maybe I'll be mentioned in the tabloid at the end of a grocery aisle. At least I'll be mentioned somewhere... Does that happen? Sure! They get more and more and more desperate... willing to try anything... when nothing works, they switch to alcolhol, then drugs and then one day they're found dead in the bathtub of their penthouse suite....

أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّهُمْ فِى كُلِّ وَادٍۢ يَهِيمُونَ

And most importantly, what does this "need for" more indicate?  It indicates that the heart is not getting what it truly needs....
Let's say you have some critical data on your phone that you need for school... You don't have a backup of it... the only original copy you have of it is on your phone.... That data is so important, that if you don't have it you can't function at school....  Now what happends when you lose your phone with all that data on it? What do you start doing? You start searching for it! Where do you look? Everywhere.... in your house... in your friends house... the places you visited... places which absolutely make no sense to search in.... but you do...  you keep searching endlessly for it.... Now I come to you and give you a brand new model of your phone... a much more beautiful, more powerful phone.... will that satisfy you? No! It does not have the content you need...
Now can I distract you temporarily... take your mind off the search for a bit.... right? I can distract you with facebook, whatsapp, hanging out with friends, PS4, Xbox etc....  but the moment the distraction is over... what do you do? You start searching for you phone again right? Now there is only one way in which this need... this search can end... What is that? It is If you find your phone.... Once you find it, will you search for it anymore? Of course not!

You see... the problem is that all these worldly luxuries can provide comfort to our bodies.... but not to our hearts... Because the heart was never meant for this world... And we constatnly try to satisfy its needs with worldly posessions... money, fame, luxuries, all sorts of jewellery and bling... But its need is not physical in nature.... The body's need is physical.... The hearts need is spiritual.... and until it finds the object of its search, the heart will always remain dissatisifed... And the heart is hungry because we have broken the rope of Allah... the عقال of Allah that he used to tie us... to keep us close to him... We broke that rope and moved away from him... And now this heart longs for Allah... it is searching for Allah... it looks for Allah every where.... wanders in every valley trying to find him... Thinks that it can replace the need for the love of Allah with gold and money, and fame and power and distractions.... but nothing satisfies it... Why doesn't it find satisfaction.... because these material things do not bring peace to the heart... Then what does? Allah answers this questions in such simple words in the Quran...
أَلَا بِذِكْرِ ٱللَّهِ تَطْمَىِٕنُّ ٱلْقُلُوبُ
Beware! Only by the rememberance of Allah, your the heart finds peace...
أَلَا بِذِكْرِ ٱللَّهِ تَطْمَىِٕنُّ ٱلْقُلُوبُ
Allah is the ultimate goal of our hearts... Until it finds Allah, the heart does not find peace... Cannot find peace... Like a fish out of water, it yearns to return back to it... our hearts crave for the love of Allah.... Like a man thirsty for water is not satisfied with anything else.... he is not distracted by anything except water...  you can give him mountains of gold and silver... all the power, fame... nothing can satisfy his thirst until he finds water....
أَلَا بِذِكْرِ ٱللَّهِ تَطْمَىِٕنُّ ٱلْقُلُوبُ

Relationship between heart and intellect

  Now let's discuss the relationship between heart and intellect...




So the intellect is a container and the heart is a container... Now if they both are containers... the question is what do they contain.... Intellect we already know is a container for what? Knowledge! Heart on the other hand is the container for spiritual components... like Faith, Tawheed etc.....

The heart is container for abstract things... while the intellect is a container for concrete things.... You understand? The heart can hold faith, trust, and emotions.... like love, anger, bravery, fear etc... And the intellect holds concrete supporting or opposing evidences... for or against those abstractions...

When we were studying the mircales of Quran.. where was the quran revealed? Heart or the intellect? On the heart!
وَإِنَّهُۥ لَتَنزِيلُ رَبِّ ٱلْعَلَمِينَ۔ نَزَلَ بِهِ ٱلرُّوحُ ٱلْأَمِينُ۔ عَلَىٰ قَلْبِكَ لِتَكُونَ مِنَ ٱلْمُنذِرِينَ۔ بِلِسَانٍ عَرَبِىٍّۢ مُّبِينٍۢ
This (Quran) has been revealed to you by the lord of the universes. Revealed through the trustworthy spirit (Jibraeel). On your heart so that you can warn mankind in clear Arabic language

what did we say contains the meanings, and what contains language? heart contains meanings, and intellect formualtes the words, correct? Now which is abstract and which is concrete? meaning is abstract and words are concrete... that's why the Quran was revealed on the heart of Rasul Allah (SAS) and not the intellect.... He used the intellect to form the words of the Quran.. So even there we see... the heart first, intellect second, right?

So my point is that the heart contains faith... and the intellect contains knowledge or information to support that faith... The more knowledge the intellect will have to support the faith... the stronger the faith will be..... The less knowledge the intellect has to support the faith... the weaker the faith will be.... You understand?

I'll give you an example ... you meet someone at a bus station... you talk to him for 10 minutes and you like that person... You don't really know the person because you met him for the first time.. but 10 minutes later, when you both walk away from each other... you leave with this fondness for that person in your heart... Do things like that happen in life? With friends, with people?
Now you start meeting him regularly at the bus station.... Now what could possibly happen to that tiny fondness in your heart? It will either grow, or it will shrink.... You will either start liking that person and he will become your friend; or you will start disliking the person and you will not want to talk to him anymore.... Question is what is going to bring about that change? Knowledge about that person! The intellect! The more you get to know that person, the more knowledge you gain about that person, the more you may like him.... or you may dislike him, depends on the supporting or opposing evidence you gather... right? So what influenced your fondness? knowledge about that person....
Now tell me..... what came first about the person? fondness or knowledge? fondness! which is related to the heart or the intellect? the heart!.... Would you have attempted to find out more about the person, or want to meet him again if you disliked the person the first time you met him? No... So Intellect came into play when? After fondness was developed! So it safe for me to say that fondness preceeded knowledge about that person? Therefore which is greater? precedence or excellence? Precedence! Which preceeded, the heart or the intellect? The heart!

So you see heart takes precedence over the intellect.... That's why it is ranked higher than intellect.... Even though intellect gave it all the support... but the one that took precedence is what? the heart!

You teach a child to say "A", the child says "A"... then you say to it, "After A comes B"... Now if the child asks why? What do you say? You say trust me! Have faith.... when a child learns anything from its parents... is it faith based or intellect based? faith based! Intellect comes way later in the game... But as he grows and gains knowledge... does his faith in the order of the alphabet increase or decrease? It increases! Would the child have been able to learn the alphabets without faith? No! Because heart comes first, then comes intellect....

In the tournament example I gave you.... did your desire to win the competition come first, or your training come first? Desire came first! Where does desire generate? in the heart or intellect? In the heart! Where does knowledge and planning for the tournament exist? In the intellect! Heart comes first... then comes intellect..

Same with anything else in life.... A movie needs to capture your heart in the first 10 minutes... doesn't it? Why? because the desire to continue needs to come first.... and then the intellect...
Think about a new book you're reading... If you get intrigued... you'll get into it... if not, you will drop it after the first chapter...
Weight loss... The heart wants to have that slice of cake even though the intellect is giving information that you have already reached your quota of calories for today... now if the heart is strong in its desire to succeed and lose weight.... it will accept the intellects recommendations... If the heart's desire to lose weight is weak.... despite all the intellectual backing.... despite all the knowledge about dieting .... will I still eat the cake? You bet I will!

So heart first! intellect later... and what comes first..... is ranked greater than what comes latter... precedence is greater than excellence.... That's why the heart is ranked higher than the intellect because of its nature of precedence... even though the intellect is very powerful and has a great influence on the decisions of the heart... but it is the heart that is in control.... intellect can give its recommendations.... if the heart agrees, it will follow it... if it doesn't it will reject it...

Think of the heart as the king... and the intellect as the wazir... Our more in our terms today... think of the heart as the President... and the intellect is the chief of staff.... Who is elected first? President or chief of staff? President! Once the president is selected... he appoints his chief of staff, correct?
But who constantly gives advise and recommendations to the president on all matters? The chief of staff! But who is in control of executing those decisions? The president!
Who completely manages, administers, and oversees the actions of the white house? Who decides who is allowed to meet the president, and who is not? Who basically does all the work? The chief of staff... All the work is done by the chief of staff... but who is recognized as the leader of the country? The president!

That's why in the hadees we learn that Allah created the intellect first... he called the intellect the most superior creation because of its excellence.... 
اول ما خلق اللہُ العقل
و عزتي و جلالي ما خلقت خلقاً اعظم منک ولا اَطوعُ منک
and then he said to the intellect... because of you I will give reward and because of you I will punish
بک أُبدي و أُعيد، لک الثواب و عليک العقاب
why? Because the intellect is the ultimate guide by Allah's design....  And notice that in Islam, until the intellect is developed... Islam is not enforced on a human being... There are 2 people exempt from Islam... the child until he reaches maturity and the insane until he gains back his sanity... What do both a child and the insane have in common? No Intellect! So Intellect is the key to entering religion....
But interesting the Quran states that when we return to Allah... Allah says I will look at what is in the heart... not what is in the intellect....
وَحُصِّلَ مَا فِى ٱلصُّدُورِ
I will bring out what is in your heart ... Why is that?
Because of the final definition of قلب , which is the essence or the innermost part of something...  In English we say the "heart" of the matter.... or the "heart" of the problem is such and such... what does that mean? It refers to the root cause of the problem.... Where the problem began..... the seed of the problem.. You fix that root cause... and the entire problem disappears... Isn;t it so?

And that's why a person is identified by the traits of his heart... When it is said that such and such is a good person, kind person, generous person, brave person.... All this qualities are actually the quality of the heart... because the heart is the true essence of a human being...  Rasul Allah said:



Indeed in the body... there is a morsel of flesh...  which, if is sound, the entire body is sound... and if it is diseased, the entire body is diseased. Truly it is the heart.

Why? Because it is the heart.... and not the intellect... that is in control.... If the heart is strong with love and kindness... and the only way it can be strong if it is backed up by strong intellectual evidence.... that to be loving and kind is the way to be, then the actions that will flow out of that person will be loving! 
If the heart is filled with hatred... and it is backed up by strong intellectual evidence that to survive one must be hateful, then the actions that will flow out of that person will be hateful! Because the heart is in control.... heart is the essence... heart is the controller...

And it is the heart where faith resides... It is the heart where Allah resides.... because it is in reality the heart that can "see" spirituality... not the intellect.... The intellect can only make sense of things by referencing things in this physical world... but the heart can see beyond the physical world.... even without intellectual backing...
Someone asked Maulana Ali... have you seen Allah? He replied... How can I worship Allah without seeing him... So he asked ... "Amir Almomineen!.. How can you see Allah?"... He replied Eyes doe not perceive him.... but hearts percieve him...

And Allah says in the Quran...
فَإِنَّهَا لَا تَعْمَى ٱلْأَبْصَرُ وَلَكِن تَعْمَى ٱلْقُلُوبُ ٱلَّتِى فِى ٱلصُّدُورِ
For indeed, it is not eyes that are blinded, but blinded are the hearts which are within the breasts.

And history is filled with great achievements that were purely faith driven! Look at the Gandhi and the Indian freedom movement... now did it make intellectual sense to stand up against the british government especially when they were in power? No! But Gandhi had faith... he had determination.. he had resolve.... all properties of the heart... but he used his intellect.. his knowledge to intelligently further his mission... he used his intellect to support his faith... but he started off with faith didn't he? What about Martin Luther King? Same thing! So the heart is the main thing because it is the true essence of a human being...

And this is what we find in the Quran in Ibrahim AS's request...
وَإِذْ قَالَ إِبْرَهِیمُ رَبِّ أَرِنِى كَيْفَ تُحْىِ ٱلْمَوْتَىٰ
And Ibrahim says, o my lord! show me how you bring the dead back to life. Allah says..
قَالَ أَوَلَمْ تُؤْمِن؟
Why Ibrahim? Don't you believe? Don't you have faith? Notice Allah asked: don't you have faith? He didn't say don't you have intellect! Where does faith reside? In the heart!  Now of course Ibrahim AS has faith... who could have more faith than Ibrahim AS... But even Ibrahim AS asks for intellectual backing....
قَالَ بَلَىٰ وَلَكِن لِّيَطْمَئِنَّ قَلْبِى
Of course I have faith! No doubt about it. But I ask you to show me so that it eases my heart. Notice he did not say لِّيَطْمَئِنَّ عقلى So that it eases my intellect! He said, لِّيَطْمَئِنَّ قَلْبِى so that it eases my heart. Allah through this incident teaches us that despite having faith, the heart can be uneasy. Until it receives knowledge about the faith... Only with intellectual evidence... faith can become strong... So then what does Allah do? He says
قَالَ فَخُذْ أَرْبَعَةًۭ مِّنَ ٱلطَّيْرِ فَصُرْهُنَّ إِلَيْكَ
Grab 4 birds and sacrifice them...
ثُمَّ ٱجْعَلْ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ جَبَلٍۢ مِّنْهُنَّ جُزْءًۭا
Then go and place the dead body of each bird on different mountains
ثُمَّ ٱدْعُهُنَّ
and then call them
يَأْتِينَكَ سَعْيًۭا ۚ
They will quicly fly back to you...
وَٱعْلَمْ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَزِيزٌ حَكِيمٌۭ
And know that Allah is most powerful and most wise...

Allah gave Ibrahim AS the intellectual physical evidence that he needed to strengthen his faith... that Allah can give life after death....


When we started studying the miracles of Quran... didn't you have faith that Quran is the word of Allah? You did... But was it backed by intellectual evidence? No! After we went through the miracles in all the various dimensions .... are you now absolutely convinced intellecutally that Quran is the word of Allah? Isn't your faith a LOT stronger now than before? So what made it stronger?


Intellect is the knowledge about how to drive a car... heart is the actual driver of the car.... The more knowledge the driver has about driving, the better he will drive the car!!
Intellect is the library... heart is the librarian!

And that's why there has to be this connection.... this balance...  between the heart and the intellect ... for Islam and Iman to become alive within us.... Heart is all about Iman... Intellect is all about Islam... The reason we find carrying out the obligations of Islam because of the weakness of Iman... weakness in faith... And why is faith weak, because it is not backed up by enough intellectual evidence for that faith to grow strong....
But then for some.... we find there is lots of intellectual knowledge... but there is no faith... it's all informative knowledge... That's what exactly my problem as I told you earlier.... I had studied so much... learnt so much... but it was all in my intellect... it was not supporting any faith in my heart... It was just to know more and show off... and win arguments and debates...... That knowledge had nothing to do with increasing my faith in Allah... or increasing Taqwa.... As a result... I could talk to you about any aspect of religion... but I was missing my prayers... doing all sorts of things...
It is only through the balance of heart and the intellect.... only through the balance of faith and knowledge to support that faith... we can move towards Allah.... Balance is the key... InshaAllah I will show you that Islam is based on balance... Balance is Allah's logo... The kalima Tawheed.... La Ilaha Illa Allah..... is all about balance.... even the letters of the kalima are balanced...

This takes us into our next topic of conversation which is the debate of science vs religion... InshaAllah we'll look at this in the next session...

What I want you to take away from today's session is...

https://katib.wordpress.com/2007/06/11/the-human-soul-and-its-dimensions/
 
NAFS (Self or EGO)

The nafs (ego), an equally essential complement to the intellect, deals with innate human desires ranging from the carnal to the spiritual.
Psychology deals with the first 2 levels of NAFS




Angels are fixed in their state. They never disobey god; they only obey God. The human being on the other hand has this interesting aspect to him or her, and that is the Human being can choose one of two things.

This is where we begin our journey. The Quran talks about two phenomena. One is DARAJAT which are upwardly. DARAJAAT means stairs moving upwards - Jacobs ladder. DARAJAAT are stairs moving down. DARAKAAT are easy; DARAJAAT are difficult. And the difference between them is the letter GEEM and the letter KA. The GEEM letter in Arabic has a harshness to it - it has a SHIDDAH, whereas the KAF letter is lighter on the tongue. The Letter KA does not have the same hardness.
A child is born in a state of FITRA, which is submission. The very first state of the child, before the NAFS begins to develop; this very  first state of the human being is submission. A scholar was once asked: how do we arrive to God? The scholar went upstairs and came down with a newborn baby and said, until you are like this, you cannot get close to God. The idea is that until you are helpless before God. Until you recognize what your human attributes are in relation to the divine attributes. Because human beings are godlike. The symmetry, the will, the volition, the power, the sense of independence, the ability to transform, the creative capacity within the human being - the human being is godlike. But he is only godlike in relation to creation. When a human being relates to self to the creator he becomes the most pathetic of creatures, he becomes the most helpless of creatures; he becomes the most incapacitated. We cannot survive a few minutes without air. There was once a Buddhist monk who was a master and he had a few disciples. One of the disciples said to him, I wish to achieve enlightenment. The monk said that the first thing you have to learn is how to breathe. The student said I already know how to do that, I've been breeding all my life. The master said, NO! You have to learn how to become conscious of your breath. He said why do have to learn that? So the monk had some of the students take him to the river at the bank of the monastery and hold him under; and then brought them up. Then he said, now do you understand why? In other words, we forget how quickly we can lose our breath. How quickly we can become helpless. Because we have the delusion of independence. And until we start becoming aware of our inherent weaknesses - the most subtle is breath itself; because we are literally one breath away from death.

So the FITRA nature is that the child is in submission to God. But then has the child begins grows, the NAFS begins to develop. And this is a very important word in the Islamic tradition. So I'll go through a little bit of a semantic field of NAFS. The root word of NAFS is NA FA SA. In Hebrew it is NAFASH, which means breath or spirit. And interestingly the Greek word psyche also means the same thing - to breathe, or related to breath.
At its base root it NAFS has 2 meanings.  NAFISA and NAFUSA. All you do is change a vowel. The first means to be precious; and the other means to be niggardly. So right there in the Arabic language, is a concept embedded in the language of "SELF". That we have two aspects to our nature. One is the beasteal and the other is the angelic. One is the selfless and the other is the niggardly. One wants to give and the other ones to hold on. And this is the essence of human dichotomy.

If you go to the next root, which is NAFFASA, where you put a SHADDA over the FA. NAFFASA  means to relieve them of their constriction. In other words to give them breath. To help them; to help someone who is restricted.
If you go to the next root NAFASA - it means to undermine somebody else; to compete against them. So it is right there in the language that human beings have these two opposing qualities.
