For the child you can see this at a very early stage. One, is that the child will smile, and it will melt the heart of its mother. Because that smile is like a gift from that baby. But it starts screaming, I WANT! So right there in a baby we see the emergence of both aspects. Negation & affirmation. Why do you think that is why do you think that is? Because this world is negation, and the next world is affirmation. Negating this world and affirming God. This is the human being. We are negators and we are affirmers. The problem is that we get it wrong. We confuse what needs to be negated with what needs to be affirmed and we end up getting into trouble.



The word NAFS is used in 3 different context in the Quran. The first is in the meaning of ZAAT or a personality. In psychology, this is referred to as the SELF or the infinite EGO. Allah has also used this word NAFS to refer to himself
وَيُحَذِّرُكُمُ ٱللَّهُ نَفْسَهُۥ
Allah warns you against Himself
كَتَبَ رَبُّكُمْ عَلَىٰ نَفْسِهِ ٱلرَّحْمَةَ
your Lord has written Mercy for Himself



NAFS AMMARA
the first NAFS is NAFS AMMARA. This means commanding self. It is the self that is commanding us to do things which we shouldn't do. And if we submit to the self, we become a slave of our passions. And the word for slave in the Arabic language is ABD. The prophet said, wretched is the slave of the DINAR and the DIRHAM; wretched is the slave of his clothes; wretched is the slave of his writing beast. Because people have this tendency to become slaves of material possessions. In psychological terms, this is the id or the libido. This is the blind soul. This is the soul that operates purely based on animal instincts. When it's hungry it will eat. This soul has no concern for what is permissible and what is forbidden. All it cares is to satisfy its hunger. It makes no difference to it whether the meat that it is eating  comes from a goat or a swipe. And even if it is eating goat, it doesn't care what kind of income was used to purchase that meat. All it really cares about is satiating its hunger. All it desires his relief۔ it's the same principle when it comes to lust. This soul operates on instant gratification. And because these instincts are completely blind, and they have absolutely no concern for what is right or wrong, the natural consequence is that they will command towards evil. This is why Allah says in the Quran:
إِنَّ ٱلنَّفْسَ لَأَمَّارَةٌۢ بِٱلسُّوٓءِ
Indeed the soul commands to evil . Then the next thing that this soul is characterized by is the urge to dominate. It wants to rule without any concern for the rights of others. This is one dimensional approach towards life.


NAFS LAWWAMA
 
this is blaming self, or the reproachful self. Here the soul is moving out of its abject nature and it is beginning to recognize a type of moral imperative. The NAFS is still doing wrong, but it is saying that I should not be doing this. This is moving out of a completely beasteal state into a moral state; and this creates a tension between the animal and the angelic side of human beings.
And this goes back to ADAM (AS) being created from earth and water. Water is a purifying element by its nature. Water in many traditions is related to consciousness. If you go to the Chinese tradition, water is related to consciousness - the element of water itself. Earth is density. Water allows light to penetrate through, but light cannot penetrate Earth. So the human being is mixed with these two admixtures of earth and water. That is one aspect of our soul but does not want to let the light through, and the other does. And this is where you go into the NAFS LAWWAMA - the reproachful self. You shouldn't have done that! This is the EGO


NAFS MUTMAINNA
and the final self is called NAFS MUTMAINNA. Which is the self at peace. This is the self that is in a state of submission. Is the self that has submitted itself completely to god. This self is no longer in a state of conflict. It's no longer fighting with itself saying that I should not do that, or I ought to do that. It has now moved into this ethical self, which has realized that this is the right thing and it wants to do it; and now it does the right thing by nature, not by compulsion - not by feeling that I have to do this thing.

So the human being has two choices. He has the choice to submit TO himself and take them into alienation, which results in the WRATH of God. To literally place himself in a relationship to God where God is completely disconnected from the soul. And this is epitomized by the idea of JAHANNUM (hell ) this is the ultimate alienation.
Now fire is a very interesting element, because it cannot rise above its nature. It has an upward motion, but it can't rise above because it is bound to the earth. You see the fire only exists because of the earth, and he had it has this upward motion. It wants to move up but it is being pulled down by the earth. And fire is very similar to light; it is very similar to the concept of light and there is light in fire. But it's not spiritual light; is the attractive attraction of lust. There is a tradition in which the prophet said, the fire is surrounded by desirable things; and paradise is surrounded by undesirable things. The idea here is, that if we look at the pure awkwardness of something, and not at its inwardness, and we submit to the outer aspects - the outer thing can look extremely desirable. But what are its inner aspects? What are its inner realities? So for instance for a man who submits to his lusts (which is a very common human experience). There is something very desirable obviously, for a male and a female to have a sexual relationship. At the physical level can be very stimulating experience. If we look at it outside of its social context, that desirability will be there. But if we place it within the context of society, what are the implications of this act? What is the implication of my sleeping with another person without any social responsibility? Because one, pregnancy can often result - so now there is a third soul that is involved - which is a result of the act. Also, what is it saying about intimacy in a culture, where the most intimate physical act becomes profane to the degree that it has absolutely no emotional content. In other words, there are implications to the act itself. If we look at it from its desirability, it's a downward motion.

If we look at the aspects that are less desirable, such as commitment - commitment is a difficult thing to do - there are many people who do not want to commit to marriage because they don't want to be bound. They find it undesirable. They want to be free. I want to be mobile; I don't want to be tied down. Footloose and fancy free; that is how I want to be! But what are the long-term effects of that? So outwardly there can be many undesirable things, but then when you look at the inner aspects of it they can be very desirable things. And so what the Quran is trying to do, is have people think deeply about things; it talks about looking at the inner realities before submitting to the outward aspects. This is why it talks about the AAQIBAH - what are the long-term effects? What is the subsequent result of the action?

The Mulsim model Is not saying that the world is bad. And the reason for that is that the world is seen as a theater of divine revelation . And the fact that God has made it a revelation means that it cannot be bad. But to the degree that God is absent from the world, the world is bad. And this is why there are two traditions of the prophet. In one he says that the world is green and beautiful; and in the other he says that the world is like a dead carcass. It's green and beautiful for the one who understands his place in the world, and honors that place. Because it is only through the acts done in this world, he becomes entitled to the success of the afterworld. He rises up to stewardship; he subdues his self; he becomes a caretaker of this own self.
But then the same world is a carcass to the one who destroys his soul in the world. And in the process ultimately he ends up destroying the world as well.


So the NAFS in a sense, is a model of human potentiality, in terms of its upward movement; and also degradation in terms of its downward movement. Most people are in the state of conflict. Many people have submitted completely to this downward motion. And there are others who are struggling to move up; and very few are actually moving up.








So Allah has used the word NAFS for himself as well.
The second context in which this word is used in the Quran, is to point out the beasteal nature of man. This is the nature that came into existence from clay.
وَلَوْ تَرَىٰٓ إِذِ ٱلظَّلِمُونَ فِى غَمَرَتِ ٱلْمَوْتِ وَٱلْمَلَٓئِكَةُ بَاسِطُوٓا۟ أَيْدِيهِمْ أَخْرِجُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَكُمُ ۖ
And if you could but see when the Zalimun are in the agonies of death, while the angels are stretching forth their hands :"Deliver your souls!


 

With regard to the nafs  (ego), Ghazali (2007, p. 8-9) describes it as a non-physical entity that fluctuates between one of three possible states as described in the Quran. The highest state is when the ego is at rest and contented due to its opposition to carnal desires. The intermediate state of self-reproachment is when the ego strives but falls short of obedience to God and remorsefully reprimands itself. The lowest state of the ego is when it easily submits to carnaldesires in opposition to God's obedience. Yusuf (2008, p. 3) also defines the nafs as the ego but says it should more appropriately refer to the tainted soul. This latter description may not be entirely accurate since the nafs as described in the Quran is a latent entity in its initial creation that has the potential to become either purified or corrupted depending on how it is disciplined by its master.
“And [by] the soul and He who proportioned it; And inspired it [with discernment of] itswickedness and its righteousness, He has succeeded who purifies it, And he has failed who instills it[with corruption].”  (Quran, 91:7-10, Saheeh International, trans.)It therefore only becomes tainted when its owner neglects to purify it and instead pursues destructive tendencies such as "pleasure, wealth, fame and power." (Yusuf, 2008, p. 3-4) It could be deduced from this that the nafs is originally pure and that only a process of constant purification can maintain it in its near pristine state.

The Ego
The nafs  (ego) as described earlier by the Quran inherently has the potential for either good or evil and the person who purifies it will succeed spiritually while the one who corrupts it will fail miserably. One of the main functions of the nafs  (ego) is to deal with desires. Desires are essential for human survival and for the pursuit of happiness. The question is whether desires could be classified as either right or wrong. In Islamic ethics there is a clear distinction between constructive and destructive desires. Pathways that are used to satisfy desires could therefore be either virtuous or wicked. Desires could become especially destructive to both the individual and the society if they are “perverted, excessive or deficient.”

It is explained that the single most important inroad to corrupting the lustful ego is the "concupiscence of the eye.” In a visualised world, air-brushed images are pervasively being used to stimulate the lower carnal appetites. He describes it as an appetite not desiring to perceive but rather to be excited. According to the Prophets it amounts to adultery of the eye and although pernicious in effect may become devastating when pervasive and chronic. The overload of sexualised images has a corroding effect on the spiritual potential of individuals. Any attempts at spiritual development through meditation and prayer are affected by corrupting images that have been allowed to enter the heart through the eyes. (Yusuf, 2008, p. 17-18)

Tazkiyatun-nafs  is the Islamically prescribed process of purification of the ego. The Arabic term tazkiyah  means to purify. It is a process whereby a person’s desires are “cleansed of all despicable characteristics.”


"Nafs" (self or ego) is the aspect of the psyche that can be viewed along a continuum, and has the potential of functioning from the grossest to the highest level. The self at its lowest level refers to our negative traits and tendencies, controlled by emotions, desires and its gratification. Sufic psychology identifies seven levels of the nafs, which have been identified in the Quran. The process of growth depends on working through these levels. These are: tyrannical self, regretful self, inspired self, serene self, pleased self, pleasing self and the pure self.[2][3]

From a Quranic perspecitve.... how can we increase our faith? How can we increase taqwa? Through Spirituality right?
Now spirituality in modern discourse was replaced by psychology. So instead of looking at things from the unseen perspective..... we want to figure them out from the seen... so if you're depressed it must have nothing to do with spirituality.... you must have a chemical imbalance in your body.. take some pills and you'll be fine... so we take human emotions and we control them with medication... For the problems of the unseen... we try to find solutions in the seen... right? Does it work? Sure! Because the soul does have an impact on the body because the metaphysical ... impacts the physical... But by negating the existence of soul ... we are actually destroying humainty.. suicide rates are at its highest in the modern world and it is in some of the wealthiest counties in the US

And this is where Quran comes into the picture so beautifully... There is a feeling.. a negative emotion that we humans have.. it's called guilt... when does someone feel guilt? When they did something wrong! or something bad... in modern psychology guilt is not a good thing... so if someone comes to a therapist and says I did something bad... I drank some alcohol... I stole some money and I feel really guilty... the therapist says to him.... you need to get rid of your guilt... you need to learn to forgive yourself... your guilt... is a human flaw inside of you... you have to accept it... become aware of it.. and get past it.... that will lead you to happiness .... You need to let go of this negative emotion.. inside of you... there is too much negativity...


Surah AlQayamah begins with the ayaats:
لَآ أُقْسِمُ بِيَوْمِ ٱلْقِيَمَةِ ‎
‏وَلَآ أُقْسِمُ بِٱلنَّفْسِ ٱللَّوَّامَةِ

Allah says... I swears by the day of judgment and I swear by the soul that feels guilt...over and over again... Lawwama means to feel guilty over and over again... continuously...

Now these 2 things qayamah and guilt in these ayats are actually laazim and malzoom.... they are tied to each other... Now the question is... what is the relationship between the day of judgment and the soul that feels guilt? Allah is telling us an incredibley spiritual and psychological reality... 

You know if you hear an alarm right now... if you heard an  announcement that there is a fire in the biulding.. please exit the building.. what will you do? You'll exit the building! the purpose of an alarm is to warn you of a danger... will you react even if you don't see the danger?... Once you hear the alarm.. even if you don't see the fire will you leave? Correct? So what was enough for you? The alarm that woke you up... Allah created every single human being with an innate sense of good.. with an innate pre-disposition towards doing the right thing... its called the fitra... and when we violate that spiriual progamming inside of us... allah created this security mechanism inside of us called ...ٱلوم ... guilt..
Guilt is actually a gift from Allah.. that what you are doing is violating your soul... and while you are violating your soul... you don't see the danger... When will you actually see the danger? When will all human beings truly see the danger of that violation that they were igorning?  On Judgment day!  The ultimate proof of judgment day is the psychological sentiment of guilt inside ourselves... the proof of judgment day is everytime you and I feel guilty..  not just muslims.. every single human being was given the gift of guilt inside of them when they are doing something wrong.. That feeling of ..... I don't know.... I feel i'm doing something wrong.. this doesn't sit well with me.. that لوم... it is an indication of qayamah...

Modern psychology cannot discuss this in a positive way.. it can't .. because for them there is no soul.. no psychological theory.. no therapist will tell you that you have to protect your heart.. we the muslims are the only ones.. we are the only one's..... this book is the only book.. this prophetic model is the only model that actually finds the balance between the seen world and the unseen world... We look at the seen world... and we can use that to connect with the unseen world... and Quran gives us the connection between the two!

The catholics embraced the unseen completely.. the protestants denied the unseen completely,  the secularists rejected the unseen entirely.. we are the ONLY religion that engages with the seen world.. and through the seen world ... we better our unseen reality...

I'll give you one example as part of this...  you know when the Budhits meditate they close their eyes... when people practice yoga and they meditate they close their eyes..  and you'll notice that in many spiritual traditions people meditate and concentrate for hours and hours with their eyes closed... Isn't that true? 
But how do we pray? eyes open! now you tell me... if you closed your eyes and prayed wouldn't that be easier? it would be! but we don't.. we keep our eyes open.... We must keep our eyes open.. it is a compulsory ettiquette of namaaz...  Why is that?  Because it is actually a fundamental concept of this religion... Because We are supposed to connect with the unseen god.. the unseen Allah.... by not losing sight of the fact that we are living in this world... even in prayer we do not get to disconnect ourselves from this world entirely...

Otherwise, why would the messenger of Allah (SAS)..... the person MOST connected to allah...  why in the world would he shorten his prayer when he hears a child cry? shouldn't he be so engrossed in prayer that he wouldnt even hear an earthquake....  let alone a child crying?  It's because We are not spiritual people at the EXPENSE of living in this world.. we have to find that balance between these two things.. this balance has to be restored... not just for ourselves but for all of humanity...  It is BECAUSE of this imbalance..... So many have rejected religion completely.. and as a reaction to it, so many have become extremists in religion... all of this... because we strayed away from the core teachings of this book.

لَآ أُقْسِمُ بِيَوْمِ ٱلْقِيَمَةِ ‎
‏وَلَآ أُقْسِمُ بِٱلنَّفْسِ ٱللَّوَّامَةِ

Subhanallah..

So I thought before we begin this conversation about science vs religion... it would really be helpful to gain some perspective on how we got to where we are... and how our religion connects the unseen world through the seen world.... and this connection is really important.... because if you can't make this connection... then there really isn't any way to find Allah.. because our diety is an unseen entity that lives in an unseen realm.... And the only way to connect to him is through the seen world.... for example we know we have a body which is seen... through that we can establish a connection with the unseen soul....  emotions are the same way... we understand emotions only when they are expressed... You cannot see love directly... because it is an unseen emotion.. If I tell you that I love you... there is no way for you to see that love in my heart... the only way you can see it..... is through my physical actions... by the things I say... through my behavior.... right?

 
Ego and Intellect Relationship

In terms of the relationship between the two, the purification of the nafs (ego) is essential to ensuring that the عقل (intellect) can function appropriately. With the intellect operating efficiently then knowledge especially about the Divine and the ruh  (spirit) can be received. (Akhir2012, p. 722)Ghazali in addition asserts that the lustful, unrestrained self or ego must be subordinate to rationality "so that its enjoyment or restraint is in accordance with one’s intelligence." (In. Yusuf,2008, p. 14) Yusuf (2008, p. 7) further elaborates that sins committed by the lustful ego may be driven by an emptiness within the person or “boredom.” Sins are therefore committed as “mere distractions to avoid confronting a lack of knowledge about life’s purpose or relevance.”
This description provides a useful insight into the connection between the nafs and the ‘aql  where a lack of knowledge may be directly responsible for indulging in carnal desires.Yusuf (2008, p. 12) argues that during the current period of moral decay where “pure lust is sought after, the venues for intellectual and spiritual pleasure are often blocked." (Yusuf, 2008, p.12) Again it becomes clearer from this argument that an over-indulgence by the lower ego will directly impair the proper rational functioning of the mind. On the other hand, Ghazali (In Yusuf, 2008, p. 14) asserts that the inculcation of modesty, a cardinal virtue emanating from a purified ego is essential to maintaining chastity. In relation to fornication of the gaze referred to before, chastity of the eyes becomes a difficult virtue. Ghazalidefines modesty as "a sense of shame in relation to blameworthy traits that are rationally discernable." Once more the intellect is shown here to play a vital role in distinguishing vice from virtue.
 
The ego and intellect are also connected in a way where knowledge of God according to a Prophetic statement is one way of purifying the ego. The Prophet Muhammad is reported to have said that purification of the ego involves knowing that God is with you wherever you may be. This knowledge of being in the presence of God, where everything that a person does is being observed and monitored, should motivate the individual to reforming his/her behaviour and actions inaccordance with Divine decree. (Zarabozo, 2002, p. 73-74)

In conclusion, the `aql  (intellect) andnafs (ego) are two vital and interconnected functions that a person can use to either purify or corrupt his/her own ruh (spirit). The intellect can be used by a person to either rise above carnality or stoop below stupidity. Similarly, the nafs (ego) has the potential for either good or evil and the one who purifies it will be successful while the one who corrupts it will fail in terms of spiritual development. The purification of the

nafs(ego) is essential to ensuring that the `aql  (intellect) can function appropriately to receive knowledge of God and the ruh

 (spirit). Together the intellect and ego must be used positively in order to develop a praiseworthy akhlaaq  (moral character) as well as an upright, righteous ruh  (spirit)


Developing a Moral Character and Upright Spirit

Appropriate use of the intellect and purification of the nafs  (ego) will lead to the formation of a praiseworthy akhlaaq

 (moral character) and thereby an upright ruh  (spirit). (Akhir, 2012, p.722) Ghazali describes the four characters of wisdom, courage, self-preservation and justice as the main prototypes for all other virtuous character traits. (In. Akhir, 2012, p. 723) Akhir (2012, p.723) explains that balancing these four characters will result in a stable spirit followed by a noble character and a sound intellect. However, based on his earlier description of the 'aql it seems that the more plausible sequence is that purification of the ego in conjunction with a sound intellect will produce a noble character and stable, upright spirit. He nonetheless eloquently asserts that if these four main characters are not in equilibrium, then despicable characteristics such as "stupidity,snobbery, short-temperedness and self-pride" will develop. He also rightly asserts that society willflourish if it "is of praiseworthy character." (Akhir, 2012, p. 723) Thus not only are there spiritualbenefits to be accrued by the individual, but there are also significant social positives to beachieved.
 
Body and soul together
 
so you have two subtle things..... the rooh and the kaseef..... the material body ..... some of the scholars have referred to it as the water in the twig ..... in other words the soul is in the body.....  but it is not in the dualist sense.....  we do not separate the two in that way  .....  which is quite often done by western civilzations.....  you cannot separate the two.....  this is why part of our Ibadah.....  is in our bodies.....  this is why we honor our bodies even when we are dead.....  we don't burn our bodies.....  so we don't feed our bodies to the  animals..... 
and because we are stuck in this material body we tend to forget.....  and this is why the Quran is a reminder..... it is a zikar.....  of what we already know.....  when we are doing Zikr Allah.....  we are  remembering what we already know.....  this is what it is .....  it's not that we are learning something new.....  it is something we already knew.....  and this is why it is called Zikrul Hakeem.....  the wise remembrance.....  and the purpose of the human being in this world is to remind ourselves of this truth and then live according to it.....  different periods of time these truths are forgotten.....  people forget and they lose their way.....  and they become our enemies.....  and that is why in the Quran Allah has warned us of these enemies.....  we have the four primary enemies of the human being..... Nafs..... Shaytan..... Hawa and Duniya..... these are the four primary enemies.....   every human being has two  struggle with these their entire lives.....  and you cannot be deluded about this..... 
 
we human beings are all in deluded states.....  and how often have I not seen things.....  that were so clearly in front of us.....  and then one day we suddenly realize it.....  something about ourselves or about somebody else.....  the blind spots..... .....  every human being has blind spots.....  there are certain things you just cannot see about yourself.....  and you don't believe them to be true even if the entire world is pointing it out to you.....  and one of the things that you cannot see is yourself.....  and that is why the Prophet said
المؤمن مرآة المؤمن
a believer is a mirror of a believer
 and what a believer reflects is not your perfection.....  what he reflects is actually your imperfections.....  and that is one of the blessings of being in the presence of the righteous people..... the salihoon..... 
 
 
یا بُنَيَّ جَالِسِ العُلَماءِ وَزاَحَمهُم بِرُكبَتَيكَ
Oh my son, sit with the scholars and put your knees up against theirs
فان اللہ یُنبِتُ فِی القُلُوبِ الحِکمَۃَ مِن جُلُوسِھِم
For Alah will grow wisdom in your heart just by your sitting with them
کما تَنبُتَ الحَبَّۃُ فی الارضِ الطَّیِّبَۃْ
Just like a seed grows in a good earth
 
so your soul is a seed..... 
قد افلح من زكاها وقد خاب من دساها
the soul needs Tazkiyah..... Tazkiyah means  to make something grow..... Yuzakkee which also means to purify.....  because something cannot grow if it's not given pure soil.....  pure water..... .pure earth.....  it needs purity to grow.....  if you put something in an environment that is not pure it will not grow.....  unless it is something befitting that environment.....  so something bad will grow in a bad environment.....  but you can't have something good grow in a bad environment..... 
 
the least we can do is at least try to be like one of these traditional Muslims who believed in these people.....  that's the least we can do..... right?
فَبِھُداھُم فَقتَدِی
 by theیر guidance follow it.....  follow their guidance..... کونو مع الصادقین .....  be with the truthful ones..... 
 
يَٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا۟ ٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَكُونُوا۟ مَعَ ٱلصَّدِقِينَ ‎
OH  people of believe.....  fear Allah and be with the truthful.....  allah tells us to be with them.....  he tells us to seek them out..... whenever they are seek them out and then sit with that.....  sit with them be with them and benefit with them.....  and be humbled before them.....

3 Parts Of The Brain

So this is the default state of the human being. This is the stage of the beast. The human brain According to modern neurology is divided into three sections. The lowest part is the R-Stem or the reptilian brain. They get these names from the evolution theory - but it is still very fascinating to look at these. Because they have serious implications for us as human beings. The reptilian brain, is the part of the human brain that deals with simple, reactive aspects of our human nature. In other words, in other words the pupils when they dilate when the light goes in, and it contracts in the presence of light; the breeding function; all of these functions that we really have no control over are governed by this RStem of the human being, we share this with the lowest of all creatures.
Now there are many human beings truly functioning at the reptilian level. They are reptiles in human form. This is really true! These are people that only know how to react. They have never given serious conscious thought to their actions. These are the types of people that will literally shoot somebody if they look at them the wrong way. It's like a snake that only knows how to strike when it feels threatened or something like that. Because all the snake has is that little R Brain.That is all it has. And there are people that function of this lowest level, in our society and other societies all over the world. And it results in complete chaos and disaster when one of these reptiles come in positions of power. Saddam Hussain is one perfect example of someone operating at this R-Stem level. If you have any knowledge about some of the heinous acts that this person committed, you would have no trouble agreeing with me. Him and his son Uday. اعوذ باللہ. Even a cobra would have a larger brain than this guy. This is a man who once broke his arm, and his friend came to the hospital to visit him, and he said to him how sad he was that his arm is broken. Uday says, are you really grieving? His friend said yes. So he says, since you are so compassionate you should feel what I am feeling right now, and he orders his bodyguard to break his friends arm - and he did that. See we cannot even comprehend somebody functioning at this level. And this man was the head of security of Saddam/s armed forces. This is a man who is subhuman. One of his hobbies was to collect sports cars. His brother-in-law had a Lamborghini that he wanted. He refused to give him that car, and Uday shot him over dinner.

So this is the picture of the R brain. The next step up is the mid brain. Because now were moving up into a level of sophistication. This is the part of the brain in which we feel emotion. This is where we experience love and hate in these types of things. And we as human beings, we share this part of the brain with higher animals. Primates have this brain. Dogs will actually get sad when their master is in trouble. Because see, a dog is an extremely loyal creature, and an extremely grateful creature. And this is interesting that loyalty and gratitude works at the level of the mid-brain. Because there are many human beings who have no problems being disloyal, and they are extremely ungrateful people. Because again these people working at the level of the R-Brain. 

The R-Stem is the state of the نفس for many people. As I explained to you earlier, the word for the intellect AQL comes from a binding court that the Arabs would use to tie their camels. Because that is the function of the intellect. It is supposed to bind us. This is what the intellect is supposed to do. It is supposed to keep us in line. It is supposed to prevent us from going astray. But if you don't use it, it is worthless. It won't do anything for you. If you do not reach this level where you are actually putting the intellect to use, then we stay at the level of a reptile, or an emotional neurotic wreck. And this is the level of the people who are operating at this base level - they are either working at the RStem level or the mid-brain (emotions). 

 

And the message of the Quran to rise above these 2 levels and get the Neocortex level.This is the level at which human beings were created to function at. Quran says AFALA TAQILOON. Why do you not use your intellect? And we have to honor our AQL by utilizing it. By not becoming some somnambulant creatures that are living life in the state of sleep walking around like everybody else. I'm going to the mall, and just watch these people walking around. It's literally the night of the living dead. You can see zombies walking around - from one shop to the other, without any purpose. They are not functioning as human beings. They are literally going through life in a state of sleep.
الناس نیام . Human beings are asleep. In other words they haven't woken up to their potential - to the full realization of who they are, and who they belong to? And what purpose they were created for! And what we need to do to become human beings. We have to enter into our state of Adamic nature. Learn by the mistakes of their parents. Parents tell you that they made these mistakes, and they tell you that because they do not want you to repeat the same mistakes. See a person who was operating at the neocortex level, will actually take advantage and benefit from that advice. When is personally operating at the RStem or midbrain level, will actually choose to repeat the same mistake for themselves. So don't operate in that lower level. Seize control of your life! We have to become people of deep thinking; we have to become people of deep reflection. We cannot be people who just waste their life away in meaningless pleasures. Don't become people of television; people of video games.

We are all born with a nature! And there are three nature's withina human being. These are called the three powers! First is قوۃ العقلیۃ.....  The rational power; the قوۃ الغضبیۃ..... The irascible power or the emotional power; and the قوۃ الشھوانیۃ, which is the power of appetite. These are the three powersthat every human being is born with, and this corresponds with modernneurological research to an extraordinary degree. We now recognize that ourbrains are tri-units. It is divided in three parts. We have the"neocortex", which is the rational brain. We have the mid-brain which is the emotional brain; and then we have the Reptilian Brain, which is the brain of appetites. And each one of these have an important function. And it'snot for nothing, that the appetite part is the base of the brain. The emotionsare in the center of the brain; but the highest is the rational brain, theneocortex. Now the way we were designed, and commanded by our Allah, was tolive in harmony with these three spheres with us. We were created to live inharmony with all three of them. But Allah has given us the AQAL, and themeaning of the word in Arabic means to constrain. And word for the intellect isthe TAMOOR (the commander). Another word is NUHAA, which is fromNAHAA, which is to forbid and to prohibit. So the rational mind is theprohibitive mind, it is the constraining aspect of the human being. It is meantto reign in. IQAAL is a hobbling chord. It is meant to rule over the midbrainand the reptilian brain. It's purpose was to hold them, and constrain them; andto use them as vehicles, in the same way that a wild horse is of no benefit toits rider. But a horse that is saddled, and bridled, and it is fed only what itneeds, so that it does not grow fat and become useless; that is the nature ofthese two lower aspects of the human being. If they are bridled and controlled,they serve us. If they become unbridled, and left without restraint, then theylord over us, and they take control of us..... 

 

 

And Allah(Subhanahu wa Ta'ala) created our bodies as representation of this DUNIYA. That's why he created it from this material world. To serve as a proof and evidence of what this worlds true nature is. It's a never ending chase

وَٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓا۟ أَعْمَلُهُمْ كَسَرَابٍۭ بِقِيعَةٍۢ يَحْسَبُهُ ٱلظَّمْـَٔانُ مَآءً حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا جَآءَهُۥ لَمْ يَجِدْهُ شَيْـًۭٔا

As for those who disbelieve, their deeds are like a mirage in a desert. The thirsty one thinks it to be water, until he comes up to it, only to find there is nothing

It's a mirage! It's a delusion of satisfaction. The desires of this DUNIYA is a bottomless pit, no matter how much to feed it, it'll never be satisfied - and the proof of that fact is our own bodies. Just look at the amount of food we have all consumed over the past 15, 20, 30, 40 years, how many chickens have we eaten, how many goats, cows, sheep have we devoured? Is the body permanently satisfied? You can stuff yourself to the point where you can no longer breathe - to the point that the mention of food makes you puke. You never want to hear the word "food" again. You are that full! How long does that feeling last? A few hours? And then what? You're hungry again and you want more. And what happens to that food we eat? That delicious food with such beautiful colors and that divine smell - what does it turn into when it comes out the other end? T

 

Default State Of The Earth

If you look at the reality of this earth by itself - in its essence it is pure darkness. I want you to imagine what the earth would be without the sun. It would be a dark cold place. Now does this earth have extremely beautiful sceneries? Like waterfalls, and mountain views? If you look at some of the natural beauty that exists on this earth, it would take your breadth away. It would reduce you to tears. But what is it that makes all of that beauty visible to us? It's light! The light of the sun. Isn't it? 

I had to visit California a couple of years go on a business trip. My hotel was a 2 hour drive from the airport. I landed there about 11 in the night, got the rental and started driving to my hotel. There was this highway I had to take for about an hour

I was once driving in California traveled somewhere late at night on a road that did not have good lighting. And all I could see was darkness outside the window. Now it so happened that my return was during the day time on the same road. And on my way back I saw these beautiful beautiful tree on both sides of the road - and this gorgeous mountain view right in front of me. It was incredible! Were these trees and mountains there when I was 

 

 

Jehad

Ruh recognizes message

Now the light of Quran falls on the intellect. It calls to a superior way of life (AKHIRA), in which the soul (NAFS) will no longer be a captive of the body, but the body will become a slave of the NAFS. The message of Quran comes with invitation of freedom; it comes with the guidelines to restore the hierarchy - in which the NAFS is superior and in control of the body. Quran comes to free the NAFS and elevate it to its full potential - to raise it in the hierarchy, to take up the ladder towards the RUH. Now when this happens, a struggle develops within the soul. Up till now, everything was good - there was no conflict. The NAFS did whatever the body commanded. But now the intellect is no longer playing along with the body. Because it has seen something greater, something superior to this DUNIYA. It is now aware of the AKHIRA - of something that is better. And the natural reaction to this awakening - this struggle - is the concept of جھاد فی سبیل اللہ. Struggling in the way of Allah. Until the Quran came there was no conflict. But now there's a clash - a conflict in the heart. A clash between what the intellect wants and what the NAFS wants. What does the intellect want? It wants to rise higher towards the spiritual world. What does the NAFS want? It wants to please the body with material possessions. And so a conflict arises which in Islamic terminology is called JIHAD. 

 

There are 2 words: جُھُد and جھاد. JOHOD means "to strive" or "to exert". JEHAD means "to struggle". With the word strive, you always find the word "for", and with struggle you always find the word "against".  You strive for something, and you struggle against something. JOHOD or strive is a uni-directional phenomenon, whereas JEHAD is a bi-directional phenomenon. If you're striving to achieve better grades, there is nothing standing against you. There is no conflict. You have a goal and you go for it by studying and working hard. But when it comes to getting the first position - the highest score - now you have other people competing for it as well. You want to get the first position and so do I. Now will will compete and struggle for each other against it. You understand? When there is no challenge, no hurdle in achieve a goal, its called JOHD, to strive for. When there is a challenge, a clash, a conflict, then there is JIHAD - a struggle against. 

Until now the body - the animal instinct was controlling the NAFS. There was no clash anywhere between the soul, the spiritual heart and the intellect. And so all components were happily striving to make the body happy. They were engaged in fulfilling all its desires and whims. Now with the message of Quran, faith came to life in the heart under the guidance of the intellect. Now there is a clash. My NAFS under the influence of my body wants to eat something - Quran says its forbidden. A desire rises within me, Allah says No! It's not allowed. There's a clash, a conflict. 

The conviction has established that there is a life after death and I will have to stand before Allah(Subhanahu wa Ta'ala) . He has sent Rasul Allah(Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam) for my guidance through this book - which has made this permissible and that forbidden. Now there is a conflict within my soul (NAFS). This struggle is inside me. It's not in my surrounding environment - its within my own personality. And this is why Rasul Allah(Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam) said, that the struggle against NAFS (struggle against the soul) is the greatest JIHAD of all. 

ایھا الجھاد افضلُ یا رسول اللہ؟ 

Oh messenger of Allah, what is the greatest of all struggles?

ان تُجاھَدَ نَفسَکَ فِی طاَعَۃِ اللہِ

To struggle against your soul in the obedience of Allah is the greatest JIHAD of all. To control it; to put reins on it and make it submit to Allah. 

المُجاھِدُ مَن جَاھَدَ نَفْسَہُ

The true MUJAHID is the one who struggles against the desires and whims of his soul. You haven't gained control over the stallion of your own NAFS and you're out to fight the enemies of Allah. Blowing up innocent poeple and killing defenseless children in the markets - What's going on? Oh I'm doing JIHAD in the way of Allah. What'll be the result? Exactly what we're seeing all around us. Millions of lives have been destroyed in the name of Islam. What good is that? It's ridiculous! The first JIHAD isthe JIHAD against the self. Take control of your own NAFS. FIRON said

وَنَادَىٰ فِرْعَوْنُ فِى قَوْمِهِۦ قَالَ يَقَوْمِ أَلَيْسَ لِى مُلْكُ مِصْرَ 

FIRON said to his people, Isn't Egypt under my dominion? 

وَهَذِهِ ٱلْأَنْهَرُ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِىٓ ۖ أَفَلَا تُبْصِرُونَ

And don't you see these rivers flowing underneath me?

أَمْ أَنَا۠ خَيْرٌۭ مِّنْ هَذَا ٱلَّذِى هُوَ مَهِينٌۭ وَلَا يَكَادُ يُبِينُ 

Am I not better this guy Moosa who can barely express himself clearly?

And this is exactly the conversation our bodies have with our NAFS. Our bodies are FIRON and our intellects are MOOSA. They body says, "am I not the one in charge of doing everything? Do I not provide you with all of these incredible pleasures? Don't I give you all the enjoyment and amusement you need? What do you need paradise? What is paradise? Isn't it pleasures anyway? I'm giving it to you right now! You're going to listen to this intellect? It can't even show you anything about the afterlife. Can you see paradise? How do you know it even exists? I'm giving it all right now, right here - cash! You're going to abandon these cash pleasures over the promise of a credit? 

فَقَالَ أَنَا۠ رَبُّكُمُ ٱلْأَعْلَىٰ

He said, I am your greatest master! You know how Allah sent MOOSA AS to FIRON to guide him to the true path? Exactly the same way Allah sent the Quran to the MOOSA of our intellects, in order to guide the FIRON of our bodies. And this is the call of Islam. This is what Rasul Allah(Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam) came for. To free the soul from the slavery of the body - to forsake it for something way better.





Quran is a book that asks many questions. It is a book that demands that human beings think. Because the nature of a question is to open the mind to reflection. So questions are an important aspect of the human mind and the human condition. And one of the questions - in fact the most important question that should come to mind is - what is a human being? We should all ask this question!

Allah(Subhanahu wa Ta'ala) gives one of the reasons of why he created the human being in teh first place. In the story of the creation of Adam AS, he shares his decision of creating man in the world.
وَإِذْ قَالَ رَبُّكَ لِلْمَلَٓئِكَةِ إِنِّى جَاعِلٌۭ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ خَلِيفَةًۭ
And when your master said to the angels: "Indeed I am going to create in the earth my caliph" -
This ISTIKHLAAF is considered the reason why Allah created human beings in the first place. He wanted to put the humano the earth as a caretaker over the earth. Not the angels, because they had seen what the JINNS had done when they were on the earth, because the presence of the jinn preceded the presence of human beings. They asked Allah(Subhanahu wa Ta'ala) are you going to put someone on this earth that is going to یفسد فیھا. That will so corruption و یسفک الدماء and who will spill blood. And what the Angels were actually saying is that we should be put on the earth as a caliph, because we will do a better job than these human beings. And Allah(Subhanahu wa Ta'ala) said انی اعلم ما لا تعلمون. I know what you don't know. When he said the statement, he was indicating that there is an element to the human nature, that was hidden from the exterior appearance of the human nature. This is the UKHRAWEE or the other worldly or the angelic element. Because it is uniquely men's position in this earth that he was created as an interspace between the two worlds. In other words, there is the angelic realm that was created for the MALAUl AALA (the highest unseen world). Because these angels are creatures that do not disobey Allah(Subhanahu wa Ta'ala). And yet it is the human being that has the ability to possess these two elements which are called the نشئتانِ,
Allah(Subhanahu wa Ta'ala) talks in the Quran about NASHATUL ULA
وَلَقَدْ عَلِمْتُمُ ٱلنَّشْأَةَ ٱلْأُولَىٰ فَلَوْلَا تَذَكَّرُونَ
And indeed, you have already known the first form of creation, why then do you not remember or take heed? This is the first creation that we are all in now۔ in another place Allah(Subhanahu wa Ta'ala) reminds us all of another creation
قُلْ سِيرُوا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ فَٱنظُرُوا۟ كَيْفَ بَدَأَ ٱلْخَلْقَ ۚ ثُمَّ ٱللَّهُ يُنشِئُ ٱلنَّشْأَةَ ٱلْآخِرَةَ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍۢ قَدِيرٌۭ
Say: "Travel in the land and see how originated creation, and then Allah will bring forth the creation of the Hereafter. Verily, Allah is Able to do all things."
So this human being has these two creations, one is this worldly creation which is the body, and the other is the next creation which is specifically for the soul. And it is this RUH and the body that have been united, in this creature that is called BANI ADAM or the INSAN. And the uniting of these two forces - these elemental forces- in which one is higher and the other is lower, Allah(Subhanahu wa Ta'ala) placed within the human being and ability to control - or be a bridge between these two elements - so that they function harmoniously and not antagonisticly with one another. And that element is called the intellect. And this is why according to the Muslim scholars a human being is called HAIWAN NATIQ - the articulating animal. And this articulation is what distinguishes us from the rest of the animal kingdom, and puts us into the angelic realm - because the Angels also have the capacity to articulate.

and so when Allah(Subhanahu wa Ta'ala) says the merciful who has revealed the Quran, has taught the Quran, created the human being and then gave him the vehicle by which you can obtain the knowledge that is in the Quran. In other words he gave him the intellect that will allow him to absorb the Quran. Because the Quran is an articulation - it is the speech of Allah(Subhanahu wa Ta'ala). And so this human being has the ability to understand speech. And Allah(Subhanahu wa Ta'ala) has spoken to the human being through the quran.

Now our lower nature is filled with two things - SHAHAWAT and GHADAB
[00:06:05]


Celestial bodies are controlling the natural events on teh earth, just as intellect is controlling the nafs and the body.


6:12 - SHAHWA from HAWA - downward movement DARAKAAT
DURRAJ is a ladder that takes effort to climb up - DARAJAT
Allah placed intellect at the top and genitals FARJ at the lowest.  Everything that exists below the private parts has a representation above - its all just muscle and bone - nothing useful is below the private parts
7:45
We share it with human beings
Animals are better
8:20 MUZMINATUZ ZINA…
Allah has placed a covenant on that organ - to be reminded that we have an oath with Allah that we will not become slaves to that lowest part of ourselves
A section is cut off from the Humana being to reject obedience to the beastly nature of human beings
These are one of the easiest inroads into the human being for SHAYTAN - to deviate human being through passion (SHAHWA) - it’s a downward motion. This passion is blocked and challenged by the intellect which is moving upwards
9:36
Intellect has been given massive faculties and capacities
There are more neurons in the brain than stars in the sky
Potential needs to be harnessed by human being
 
Human being also has capacity of self deception which is essential for its survival. The first deception is that  he thinks he will not die. Which is important for a child to grow.
This state of believing immortality is important for child to experience the world. That allows it to experience the world. If a child realized how dangerous the world is, it would not get out of bed
Child believes in self deluded sense that it is not vulenerable.
A child will touch fire and get burnt. And it learns a lesson that gets imprinted in the brain - at the cellular level.
It will bruise itself and learn that it can get hurt. It learns its limitations
It is a movement from deluded state (without limits) to understanding that it has limits. These are pre-requisites to understanding the limits of Allah
Just as there are physical limits to the body, there are moral limits that if transgressed then things will happen just like in the physical dimension.
This is a world of limitations - We live in a world that propagates the idea of no limits
SHAYTAN wants us to break limits
He complained about clay and considered it lower than fire 13:30

 

So after 120 days in the mother's womb, Allah connects the RUH with the body, and the fetus becomes alive. So if you imagine the body as an empty dark room - the lights have now been turned on. The internal physical machinery of the body has been activated. The fetus is now alive. 
غَدَیتَنِی فِی الحَاشَ جَنِیَّا ؛ وَکُنتَ لِی وَالِدَیَّ
You nourished me in the womb of my mother as an embryo; and I belong to you before I belonged to my parents
خَلَقتَنِی مُسلِماً وَلَو لا فَضلُکَ لَم أَعرِفٌ النَّبِیَّ
You are the one who created me a Muslim; and if it wasn't for your blessing I would never have know this beloved prophet (Salallahu alayhi wa aalihee wa sallam) 
So in these 9 months, there is a massive transformation from a uni-cellular entity to this extremely complicated creature. That literally has a brain and these incredibly intricate organs. And I encourage everyone one in this group to study basic physiology. Just understanding the intricacies of these organs will overwhelm you. It is phenomenal! Something beyond comprehension. The way that our entire nervous system works. The way in which we have volition. The way in which the brain functions.



Crisis of knowledge
But today if you look at the world there are two powers that are ruling.....  there is the irascible power and the concupiscent  and power..... the dog soul and the pig soul..... these are the two powers that are reigning in this world today.....  people that are consuming and are not recognizing that their consumption is killing them.....  and then there are people that are affecting the rage on the world.....  literally going out with rage in the world.....  the warmongers..... .....  whether  they are spreading the weapons of war..... .....  or whether they are using it themselves.....  it is prohibited in Islam to sell weapons during the time of war.....  because Islam does not encourage fighting.....  Islam discourages fighting and tries to put an end to it..... Muslims are supposed to want to put the flames war out.....  not to ignite the flames of war.....
